                                                                  {
  "content": "\"\"\"\nRouter pour la gestion des flashcards avec revision espacee (algorithme SM-2).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException\nfrom sqlalchemy import select\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\n\nfrom app.database import get_db\nfrom app.models.flashcard import Flashcard\nfrom app.schemas.flashcard import FlashcardCreate, FlashcardResponse, FlashcardReview\n\nrouter = APIRouter()\n\n\n@router.post(\"/\", response_model=FlashcardResponse)\nasync def create_flashcard(\n    flashcard_in: FlashcardCreate,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Cree une nouvelle flashcard.\"\"\"\n    flashcard = Flashcard(\n        question=flashcard_in.question,\n        reponse=flashcard_in.reponse,\n        explication=flashcard_in.explication,\n        difficulte=flashcard_in.difficulte,\n        matiere_id=flashcard_in.matiere_id,\n        document_id=flashcard_in.document_id,\n    )\n\n    db.add(flashcard)\n    await db.commit()\n    await db.refresh(flashcard)\n\n    return flashcard\n\n\n@router.get(\"/\", response_model=list[FlashcardResponse])\nasync def list_flashcards(\n    matiere_id: Optional[int] = None,\n    document_id: Optional[int] = None,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Liste les flashcards avec filtres optionnels par matiere et/ou document.\"\"\"\n    query = select(Flashcard).order_by(Flashcard.created_at.desc())\n\n    if matiere_id is not None:\n        query = query.where(Flashcard.matiere_id == matiere_id)\n    if document_id is not None:\n        query = query.where(Flashcard.document_id == document_id)\n\n    result = await db.execute(query)\n    flashcards = result.scalars().all()\n    return flashcards\n\n\n@router.get(\"/revision\", response_model=list[FlashcardResponse])\nasync def get_revision_flashcards(\n    matiere_id: Optional[int] = None,\n    limit: int = 20,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Recupere les flashcards dont la prochaine revision est passee ou aujourd'hui.\n    Triees par date de revision la plus ancienne en premier (les plus urgentes).\n    \"\"\"\n    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    query = (\n        select(Flashcard)\n        .where(Flashcard.prochaine_revision <= now)\n        .order_by(Flashcard.prochaine_revision.asc())\n        .limit(limit)\n    )\n\n    if matiere_id is not None:\n        query = query.where(Flashcard.matiere_id == matiere_id)\n\n    result = await db.execute(query)\n    flashcards = result.scalars().all()\n    return flashcards\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{flashcard_id}/review\", response_model=FlashcardResponse)\nasync def review_flashcard(\n    flashcard_id: int,\n    review: FlashcardReview,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Met a jour une flashcard apres revision en utilisant l'algorithme SM-2.\n\n    Le champ `qualite` (0-5) represente la qualite de la reponse:\n        - 0: Aucun souvenir\n        - 1: Mauvaise reponse, mais reconnait la bonne\n        - 2: Mauvaise reponse, mais la bonne semble facile a retenir\n        - 3: Bonne reponse avec difficulte importante\n        - 4: Bonne reponse apres hesitation\n        - 5: Reponse parfaite\n    \"\"\"\n    if not 0 <= review.qualite <= 5:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"La qualite doit etre entre 0 et 5\"\n        )\n\n    result = await db.execute(\n        select(Flashcard).where(Flashcard.id == flashcard_id)\n    )\n    flashcard = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n    if flashcard is None:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Flashcard non trouvee\")\n\n    # --- Algorithme SM-2 ---\n    q = review.qualite\n    ef = flashcard.facteur_facilite\n    reps = flashcard.repetitions\n    interval = flashcard.intervalle_jours\n\n    # Nouveau facteur de facilite (EF)\n    # EF' = EF + (0.1 - (5 - q) * (0.08 + (5 - q) * 0.02))\n    new_ef = ef + (0.1 - (5 - q) * (0.08 + (5 - q) * 0.02))\n    # EF ne doit jamais descendre en dessous de 1.3\n    new_ef = max(1.3, new_ef)\n\n    if q < 3:\n        # Reponse incorrecte : on recommence les repetitions\n        new_reps = 0\n        new_interval = 1.0\n    else:\n        # Reponse correcte\n        if reps == 0:\n            new_interval = 1.0\n        elif reps == 1:\n            new_interval = 6.0\n        else:\n            new_interval = interval * new_ef\n\n        new_reps = reps + 1\n\n    # Calculer la prochaine date de revision\n    prochaine_revision = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=new_interval)\n\n    # Mettre a jour la flashcard\n    flashcard.facteur_facilite = round(new_ef, 2)\n    flashcard.repetitions = new_reps\n    flashcard.intervalle_jours = round(new_interval, 1)\n    flashcard.prochaine_revision = prochaine_revision\n\n    await db.commit()\n    await db.refresh(flashcard)\n\n    return flashcard\n\n\n@router.get(\"/{flashcard_id}\", response_model=FlashcardResponse)\nasync def get_flashcard(\n    flashcard_id: int,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Recupere une flashcard par son ID.\"\"\"\n    result = await db.execute(\n        select(Flashcard).where(Flashcard.id == flashcard_id)\n    )\n    flashcard = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n    if flashcard is None:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Flashcard non trouvee\")\n\n    return flashcard\n\n\n@router.delete(\"/{flashcard_id}\")\nasync def delete_flashcard(\n    flashcard_id: int,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Supprime une flashcard.\"\"\"\n    result = await db.execute(\n        select(Flashcard).where(Flashcard.id == flashcard_id)\n    )\n    flashcard = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n    if flashcard is None:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Flashcard non trouvee\")\n\n    await db.delete(flashcard)\n    await db.commit()\n\n    return {\"message\": \"Flashcard supprimee\", \"id\": flashcard_id}\n",
  "file_path": "/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/routers/flashcards.py"
}
                                                                  ```

                                                                      {
  "content": "\"\"\"\nRouter pour la gestion des quiz et la soumission de reponses.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException\nfrom sqlalchemy import select\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload\n\nfrom app.database import get_db\nfrom app.models.quiz import Quiz, QuizQuestion\nfrom app.schemas.quiz import (\n    QuizCreate,\n    QuizResponse,\n    QuizSubmission,\n    QuizResult,\n)\n\nrouter = APIRouter()\n\n\n@router.post(\"/\", response_model=QuizResponse)\nasync def create_quiz(\n    quiz_in: QuizCreate,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Cree un nouveau quiz avec ses questions.\"\"\"\n    quiz = Quiz(\n        titre=quiz_in.titre,\n        matiere_id=quiz_in.matiere_id,\n        document_id=quiz_in.document_id,\n    )\n\n    # Creer les questions associees\n    for q_data in quiz_in.questions:\n        question = QuizQuestion(\n            question=q_data.question,\n            choix=q_data.choix,\n            reponse_correcte=q_data.reponse_correcte,\n            explication=q_data.explication,\n            difficulte=q_data.difficulte,\n        )\n        quiz.questions.append(question)\n\n    db.add(quiz)\n    await db.commit()\n    await db.refresh(quiz)\n\n    # Recharger avec les questions (eager loading)\n    result = await db.execute(\n        select(Quiz)\n        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))\n        .where(Quiz.id == quiz.id)\n    )\n    quiz = result.scalar_one()\n\n    return quiz\n\n\n@router.get(\"/\", response_model=list[QuizResponse])\nasync def list_quizzes(\n    matiere_id: Optional[int] = None,\n    document_id: Optional[int] = None,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Liste tous les quiz avec filtres optionnels.\"\"\"\n    query = (\n        select(Quiz)\n        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))\n        .order_by(Quiz.created_at.desc())\n    )\n\n    if matiere_id is not None:\n        query = query.where(Quiz.matiere_id == matiere_id)\n    if document_id is not None:\n        query = query.where(Quiz.document_id == document_id)\n\n    result = await db.execute(query)\n    quizzes = result.scalars().unique().all()\n    return quizzes\n\n\n@router.get(\"/{quiz_id}\", response_model=QuizResponse)\nasync def get_quiz(\n    quiz_id: int,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Recupere un quiz par son ID avec ses questions.\"\"\"\n    result = await db.execute(\n        select(Quiz)\n        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))\n        .where(Quiz.id == quiz_id)\n    )\n    quiz = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n    if quiz is None:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Quiz non trouve\")\n\n    return quiz\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{quiz_id}/submit\", response_model=QuizResult)\nasync def submit_quiz(\n    quiz_id: int,\n    submission: QuizSubmission,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Soumet les reponses a un quiz et calcule le score.\n\n    Chaque reponse contient le question_id et l'index de la reponse choisie.\n    Retourne le score, le total et les details par question.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Charger le quiz avec ses questions\n    result = await db.execute(\n        select(Quiz)\n        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))\n        .where(Quiz.id == quiz_id)\n    )\n    quiz = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n    if quiz is None:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Quiz non trouve\")\n\n    # Indexer les questions par ID pour un acces rapide\n    questions_map = {q.id: q for q in quiz.questions}\n\n    score = 0\n    total = len(quiz.questions)\n    details: list[dict] = []\n\n    for answer in submission.reponses:\n        question = questions_map.get(answer.question_id)\n        if question is None:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Question {answer.question_id} n'appartient pas a ce quiz\",\n            )\n\n        is_correct = answer.reponse == question.reponse_correcte\n        if is_correct:\n            score += 1\n\n        details.append(\n            {\n                \"question_id\": question.id,\n                \"question\": question.question,\n                \"reponse_donnee\": answer.reponse,\n                \"reponse_correcte\": question.reponse_correcte,\n                \"correct\": is_correct,\n                \"explication\": question.explication,\n            }\n        )\n\n    return QuizResult(score=score, total=total, details=details)\n\n\n@router.delete(\"/{quiz_id}\")\nasync def delete_quiz(\n    quiz_id: int,\n    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),\n):\n    \"\"\"Supprime un quiz et toutes ses questions (cascade).\"\"\"\n    result = await db.execute(select(Quiz).where(Quiz.id == quiz_id))\n    quiz = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n    if quiz is None:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Quiz non trouve\")\n\n    await db.delete(quiz)\n    await db.commit()\n\n    return {\"message\": \"Quiz supprime\", \"id\": quiz_id}\n",
  "file_path": "/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/routers/quiz.py"
}
                                                                      ```

---

## Resume des fichiers crees

### 1. `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/services/document_parser.py` (S8)

Service d'extraction de texte avec la fonction `async parse_document(file_path: str) -> str` :

- **PDF** : extraction via `pymupdf` (import fitz), page par page avec `page.get_text()`
- **PPTX** : extraction via `python-pptx`, parcourt chaque slide et chaque shape avec text_frame
- **DOCX** : extraction via `python-docx`, parcourt chaque paragraphe
- Verification que le fichier existe, detection automatique du type par extension
- Les fonctions d'extraction internes (`_extract_pdf`, `_extract_pptx`, `_extract_docx`) sont synchrones mais encapsulees dans une fonction async

### 2. `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/routers/documents.py` (S5)

APIRouter avec prefixe `""` (le prefixe `/api/documents` est deja applique dans `main.py`) :

- **`POST /upload`** : Upload de fichier via `UploadFile` + champs `Form`. Valide l'extension (pdf/pptx/docx), sauvegarde dans `settings.upload_path`, extrait le texte via `parse_document`, cree l'enregistrement en base
- **`GET /`** : Liste tous les documents, filtre optionnel par `matiere_id`
- **`GET /{document_id}`** : Recupere un document par son ID
- **`DELETE /{document_id}`** : Supprime le document en base et le fichier physique

### 3. `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/routers/flashcards.py` (S6)

Endpoints pour les flashcards avec algorithme SM-2 :

- **`POST /`** : Cree une flashcard
- **`GET /`** : Liste avec filtres optionnels par `matiere_id` et `document_id`
- **`GET /revision`** : Retourne les flashcards dont `prochaine_revision <= now`, triees par urgence, avec limite configurable
- **`POST /{flashcard_id}/review`** : Implementation complete de l'algorithme SM-2 :
  - Qualite de 0 a 5
  - Nouveau facteur de facilite : `EF' = EF + (0.1 - (5-q) * (0.08 + (5-q) * 0.02))`, minimum 1.3
  - Si qualite < 3 : reset repetitions a 0, intervalle a 1 jour
  - Si qualite >= 3 : intervalle = 1j (rep 0), 6j (rep 1), puis `interval * EF` (rep 2+)
  - Calcul de `prochaine_revision = now + timedelta(days=new_interval)`
- **`GET /{flashcard_id}`** : Recupere une flashcard par son ID
- **`DELETE /{flashcard_id}`** : Supprime une flashcard

Note : l'endpoint `GET /revision` est declare **avant** `GET /{flashcard_id}` pour eviter que FastAPI ne le confonde avec un ID.

### 4. `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/routers/quiz.py` (S7)

Endpoints pour les quiz :

- **`POST /`** : Cree un quiz avec ses questions en une seule requete (utilise la relation `quiz.questions.append()`)
- **`GET /`** : Liste avec filtres, utilise `selectinload(Quiz.questions)` pour charger les questions en eager loading
- **`GET /{quiz_id}`** : Recupere un quiz avec toutes ses questions
- **`POST /{quiz_id}/submit`** : Soumet les reponses, calcule le score. Pour chaque reponse, verifie que la question appartient au quiz, compare `answer.reponse` avec `question.reponse_correcte`, retourne un `QuizResult` avec score, total, et details par question
- **`DELETE /{quiz_id}`** : Supprime le quiz (cascade sur les questions via la relation SQLAlchemy)

### 5. `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/routers/__init__.py` (mise a jour)

```python
from app.routers import documents, flashcards, quiz

__all__ = ["documents", "flashcards", "quiz"]
```

---

## Points techniques

- **Async/await partout** : toutes les fonctions de route utilisent `async def`, les sessions DB utilisent `await db.execute()`, `await db.commit()`, `await db.refresh()`
- **`from sqlalchemy import select`** : utilise partout pour les requetes au lieu de l'ancienne API `session.query()`
- **`selectinload`** : utilise dans le router quiz pour charger les relations `questions` en eager loading (necessaire en mode async SQLAlchemy)
- **Coherence avec `main.py`** : les routers utilisent `router = APIRouter()` sans prefixe, car les prefixes `/api/documents`, `/api/flashcards`, `/api/quiz` sont deja definis dans `main.py` via `app.include_router()`

     2→from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase
     3→
     4→from app.config import settings
     5→
     6→DATABASE_URL = settings.database_url.replace("sqlite:///", "sqlite+aiosqlite:///")

     9→
    10→async_session = async_sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)

    14→    pass
    15→
    16→
    17→async def get_db():
    18→    async with async_session() as session:
    19→        yield session

                                                                     1→"""
     2→Router pour la gestion des flashcards avec revision espacee (algorithme SM-2).
     3→"""
     4→
     5→import math
     6→from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
     7→from typing import Optional
     8→
     9→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
    10→from sqlalchemy import select, func
    11→from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
    12→
    13→from app.database import get_db
    14→from app.models.flashcard import Flashcard
    15→from app.schemas.flashcard import FlashcardCreate, FlashcardResponse, FlashcardReview
    16→
    17→router = APIRouter()
    18→
    19→
    20→@router.post("", response_model=FlashcardResponse)
    21→async def create_flashcard(
    22→    flashcard_in: FlashcardCreate,
    23→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    24→):
    25→    """Cree une nouvelle flashcard."""
    26→    flashcard = Flashcard(
    27→        question=flashcard_in.question,
    28→        reponse=flashcard_in.reponse,
    29→        explication=flashcard_in.explication,
    30→        difficulte=flashcard_in.difficulte,
    31→        matiere_id=flashcard_in.matiere_id,
    32→        document_id=flashcard_in.document_id,
    33→    )
    34→
    35→    db.add(flashcard)
    36→    await db.commit()
    37→    await db.refresh(flashcard)
    38→
    39→    return flashcard
    40→
    41→
    42→@router.get("", response_model=list[FlashcardResponse])
    43→async def list_flashcards(
    44→    matiere_id: Optional[int] = None,
    45→    document_id: Optional[int] = None,
    46→    limit: int = 200,
    47→    offset: int = 0,
    48→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    49→):
    50→    """Liste les flashcards avec filtres optionnels par matiere et/ou document.
    51→    Sans filtre de matiere, applique un interleaving equilivre multi-matieres.
    52→    """
    53→    if matiere_id is not None or document_id is not None:
    54→        query = select(Flashcard).order_by(Flashcard.created_at.desc())
    55→        if matiere_id is not None:
    56→            query = query.where(Flashcard.matiere_id == matiere_id)
    57→        if document_id is not None:
    58→            query = query.where(Flashcard.document_id == document_id)
    59→        if offset > 0:
    60→            query = query.offset(offset)
    61→        if limit > 0:
    62→            query = query.limit(limit)
    63→        result = await db.execute(query)
    64→        return result.scalars().all()
    65→
    66→    # Mode equilivre : interleaving par matiere
    67→    matieres_result = await db.execute(
    68→        select(Flashcard.matiere_id).distinct()
    69→    )
    70→    matiere_ids = [row[0] for row in matieres_result.all()]
    71→
    72→    if not matiere_ids:
    73→        return []
    74→
    75→    effective_limit = limit if limit > 0 else 200
    76→    per_matiere = max(1, math.ceil(effective_limit / len(matiere_ids)))
    77→
    78→    buckets: list[list[Flashcard]] = []
    79→    for mid in matiere_ids:
    80→        q = (
    81→            select(Flashcard)
    82→            .where(Flashcard.matiere_id == mid)
    83→            .order_by(func.random())
    84→            .limit(per_matiere)
    85→        )
    86→        res = await db.execute(q)
    87→        buckets.append(list(res.scalars().all()))
    88→
    89→    interleaved: list[Flashcard] = []
    90→    max_len = max(len(b) for b in buckets)
    91→    for i in range(max_len):
    92→        for bucket in buckets:
    93→            if i < len(bucket):
    94→                interleaved.append(bucket[i])
    95→
    96→    start = offset if offset > 0 else 0
    97→    return interleaved[start: start + effective_limit]
    98→
    99→
   100→@router.get("/revision", response_model=list[FlashcardResponse])
   101→async def get_revision_flashcards(
   102→    matiere_id: Optional[int] = None,
   103→    limit: int = 200,
   104→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   105→):
   106→    """
   107→    Recupere les flashcards dont la prochaine revision est passee ou aujourd'hui.
   108→    Triees par date de revision la plus ancienne en premier (les plus urgentes).
   109→    """
   110→    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   111→    query = (
   112→        select(Flashcard)
   113→        .where(Flashcard.prochaine_revision <= now)
   114→        .order_by(Flashcard.prochaine_revision.asc())
   115→        .limit(limit)
   116→    )
   117→
   118→    if matiere_id is not None:
   119→        query = query.where(Flashcard.matiere_id == matiere_id)
   120→
   121→    result = await db.execute(query)
   122→    flashcards = result.scalars().all()
   123→    return flashcards
   124→
   125→
   126→@router.post("/{flashcard_id}/review", response_model=FlashcardResponse)
   127→async def review_flashcard(
   128→    flashcard_id: int,
   129→    review: FlashcardReview,
   130→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   131→):
   132→    """
   133→    Met a jour une flashcard apres revision en utilisant l'algorithme SM-2.
   134→
   135→    Le champ `qualite` (0-5) represente la qualite de la reponse:
   136→        - 0: Aucun souvenir
   137→        - 1: Mauvaise reponse, mais reconnait la bonne
   138→        - 2: Mauvaise reponse, mais la bonne semble facile a retenir
   139→        - 3: Bonne reponse avec difficulte importante
   140→        - 4: Bonne reponse apres hesitation
   141→        - 5: Reponse parfaite
   142→    """
   143→    if not 0 <= review.qualite <= 5:
   144→        raise HTTPException(
   145→            status_code=400, detail="La qualite doit etre entre 0 et 5"
   146→        )
   147→
   148→    result = await db.execute(
   149→        select(Flashcard).where(Flashcard.id == flashcard_id)
   150→    )
   151→    flashcard = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   152→
   153→    if flashcard is None:
   154→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Flashcard non trouvee")
   155→
   156→    # --- Algorithme SM-2 ---
   157→    q = review.qualite
   158→    ef = flashcard.facteur_facilite
   159→    reps = flashcard.repetitions
   160→    interval = flashcard.intervalle_jours
   161→
   162→    # Nouveau facteur de facilite (EF)
   163→    # EF' = EF + (0.1 - (5 - q) * (0.08 + (5 - q) * 0.02))
   164→    new_ef = ef + (0.1 - (5 - q) * (0.08 + (5 - q) * 0.02))
   165→    # EF ne doit jamais descendre en dessous de 1.3
   166→    new_ef = max(1.3, new_ef)
   167→
   168→    if q < 3:
   169→        # Reponse incorrecte : on recommence les repetitions
   170→        new_reps = 0
   171→        new_interval = 1.0
   172→    else:
   173→        # Reponse correcte
   174→        if reps == 0:
   175→            new_interval = 1.0
   176→        elif reps == 1:
   177→            new_interval = 6.0
   178→        else:
   179→            new_interval = interval * new_ef
   180→
   181→        new_reps = reps + 1
   182→
   183→    # Calculer la prochaine date de revision
   184→    prochaine_revision = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=new_interval)
   185→
   186→    # Mettre a jour la flashcard
   187→    flashcard.facteur_facilite = round(new_ef, 2)
   188→    flashcard.repetitions = new_reps
   189→    flashcard.intervalle_jours = round(new_interval, 1)
   190→    flashcard.prochaine_revision = prochaine_revision
   191→
   192→    await db.commit()
   193→    await db.refresh(flashcard)
   194→
   195→    return flashcard
   196→
   197→
   198→@router.get("/{flashcard_id}", response_model=FlashcardResponse)
   199→async def get_flashcard(
   200→    flashcard_id: int,
   201→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   202→):
   203→    """Recupere une flashcard par son ID."""
   204→    result = await db.execute(
   205→        select(Flashcard).where(Flashcard.id == flashcard_id)
   206→    )
   207→    flashcard = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   208→
   209→    if flashcard is None:
   210→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Flashcard non trouvee")
   211→
   212→    return flashcard
   213→
   214→
   215→@router.delete("/{flashcard_id}")
   216→async def delete_flashcard(
   217→    flashcard_id: int,
   218→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   219→):
   220→    """Supprime une flashcard."""
   221→    result = await db.execute(
   222→        select(Flashcard).where(Flashcard.id == flashcard_id)
   223→    )
   224→    flashcard = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   225→
   226→    if flashcard is None:
   227→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Flashcard non trouvee")
   228→
   229→    await db.delete(flashcard)
   230→    await db.commit()
   231→
   232→    return {"message": "Flashcard supprimee", "id": flashcard_id}
   233→
                                                                ```

                                                                      ```
                                                                           1→"""
     2→Router pour la gestion des quiz et la soumission de reponses.
     3→"""
     4→
     5→import math
     6→from typing import Optional
     7→
     8→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
     9→from sqlalchemy import select, func
    10→from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
    11→from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload
    12→
    13→from app.database import get_db
    14→from app.models.quiz import Quiz, QuizQuestion
    15→from app.schemas.quiz import (
    16→    QuizCreate,
    17→    QuizResponse,
    18→    QuizSubmission,
    19→    QuizResult,
    20→)
    21→
    22→router = APIRouter()
    23→
    24→
    25→@router.post("", response_model=QuizResponse)
    26→async def create_quiz(
    27→    quiz_in: QuizCreate,
    28→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    29→):
    30→    """Cree un nouveau quiz avec ses questions."""
    31→    quiz = Quiz(
    32→        titre=quiz_in.titre,
    33→        matiere_id=quiz_in.matiere_id,
    34→        document_id=quiz_in.document_id,
    35→    )
    36→
    37→    # Creer les questions associees
    38→    for q_data in quiz_in.questions:
    39→        question = QuizQuestion(
    40→            question=q_data.question,
    41→            choix=q_data.choix,
    42→            reponse_correcte=q_data.reponse_correcte,
    43→            explication=q_data.explication,
    44→            difficulte=q_data.difficulte,
    45→        )
    46→        quiz.questions.append(question)
    47→
    48→    db.add(quiz)
    49→    await db.commit()
    50→    await db.refresh(quiz)
    51→
    52→    # Recharger avec les questions (eager loading)
    53→    result = await db.execute(
    54→        select(Quiz)
    55→        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))
    56→        .where(Quiz.id == quiz.id)
    57→    )
    58→    quiz = result.scalar_one()
    59→
    60→    return quiz
    61→
    62→
    63→@router.get("", response_model=list[QuizResponse])
    64→async def list_quizzes(
    65→    matiere_id: Optional[int] = None,
    66→    document_id: Optional[int] = None,
    67→    limit: int = 200,
    68→    offset: int = 0,
    69→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    70→):
    71→    """Liste tous les quiz avec filtres optionnels.
    72→    Quand aucune matiere_id n'est specifiee, applique un interleaving
    73→    equilbre multi-matieres pour eviter le biais vers une seule matiere.
    74→    """
    75→    if matiere_id is not None or document_id is not None:
    76→        # Filtre explicite : comportement normal
    77→        query = (
    78→            select(Quiz)
    79→            .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))
    80→            .order_by(Quiz.created_at.desc())
    81→        )
    82→        if matiere_id is not None:
    83→            query = query.where(Quiz.matiere_id == matiere_id)
    84→        if document_id is not None:
    85→            query = query.where(Quiz.document_id == document_id)
    86→        if offset > 0:
    87→            query = query.offset(offset)
    88→        if limit > 0:
    89→            query = query.limit(limit)
    90→        result = await db.execute(query)
    91→        return result.scalars().unique().all()
    92→
    93→    # Mode equilbre : interleaving par matiere
    94→    # Recuperer les matieres distinctes presentes
    95→    matieres_result = await db.execute(
    96→        select(Quiz.matiere_id).distinct()
    97→    )
    98→    matiere_ids = [row[0] for row in matieres_result.all()]
    99→
   100→    if not matiere_ids:
   101→        return []
   102→
   103→    effective_limit = limit if limit > 0 else 200
   104→    per_matiere = max(1, math.ceil(effective_limit / len(matiere_ids)))
   105→
   106→    buckets: list[list[Quiz]] = []
   107→    for mid in matiere_ids:
   108→        q = (
   109→            select(Quiz)
   110→            .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))
   111→            .where(Quiz.matiere_id == mid)
   112→            .order_by(func.random())
   113→            .limit(per_matiere)
   114→        )
   115→        res = await db.execute(q)
   116→        buckets.append(list(res.scalars().unique().all()))
   117→
   118→    # Interleave : round-robin sur les buckets
   119→    interleaved: list[Quiz] = []
   120→    max_len = max(len(b) for b in buckets)
   121→    for i in range(max_len):
   122→        for bucket in buckets:
   123→            if i < len(bucket):
   124→                interleaved.append(bucket[i])
   125→
   126→    # Appliquer offset + limit finaux
   127→    start = offset if offset > 0 else 0
   128→    return interleaved[start: start + effective_limit]
   129→
   130→
   131→@router.get("/{quiz_id}", response_model=QuizResponse)
   132→async def get_quiz(
   133→    quiz_id: int,
   134→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   135→):
   136→    """Recupere un quiz par son ID avec ses questions."""
   137→    result = await db.execute(
   138→        select(Quiz)
   139→        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))
   140→        .where(Quiz.id == quiz_id)
   141→    )
   142→    quiz = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   143→
   144→    if quiz is None:
   145→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Quiz non trouve")
   146→
   147→    return quiz
   148→
   149→
   150→@router.post("/{quiz_id}/submit", response_model=QuizResult)
   151→async def submit_quiz(
   152→    quiz_id: int,
   153→    submission: QuizSubmission,
   154→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   155→):
   156→    """
   157→    Soumet les reponses a un quiz et calcule le score.
   158→
   159→    Chaque reponse contient le question_id et l'index de la reponse choisie.
   160→    Retourne le score, le total et les details par question.
   161→    """
   162→    # Charger le quiz avec ses questions
   163→    result = await db.execute(
   164→        select(Quiz)
   165→        .options(selectinload(Quiz.questions))
   166→        .where(Quiz.id == quiz_id)
   167→    )
   168→    quiz = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   169→
   170→    if quiz is None:
   171→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Quiz non trouve")
   172→
   173→    # Indexer les questions par ID pour un acces rapide
   174→    questions_map = {q.id: q for q in quiz.questions}
   175→
   176→    score = 0
   177→    total = len(quiz.questions)
   178→    details: list[dict] = []
   179→
   180→    for answer in submission.reponses:
   181→        question = questions_map.get(answer.question_id)
   182→        if question is None:
   183→            raise HTTPException(
   184→                status_code=400,
   185→                detail=f"Question {answer.question_id} n'appartient pas a ce quiz",
   186→            )
   187→
   188→        is_correct = answer.reponse == question.reponse_correcte
   189→        if is_correct:
   190→            score += 1
   191→
   192→        details.append(
   193→            {
   194→                "question_id": question.id,
   195→                "question": question.question,
   196→                "reponse_donnee": answer.reponse,
   197→                "reponse_correcte": question.reponse_correcte,
   198→                "correct": is_correct,
   199→                "explication": question.explication,
   200→            }
   201→        )
   202→
   203→    return QuizResult(score=score, total=total, details=details)
   204→
   205→
   206→@router.delete("/{quiz_id}")
   207→async def delete_quiz(
   208→    quiz_id: int,
   209→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   210→):
   211→    """Supprime un quiz et toutes ses questions (cascade)."""
   212→    result = await db.execute(select(Quiz).where(Quiz.id == quiz_id))
   213→    quiz = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   214→
   215→    if quiz is None:
   216→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Quiz non trouve")
   217→
   218→    await db.delete(quiz)
   219→    await db.commit()
   220→
   221→    return {"message": "Quiz supprime", "id": quiz_id}
   222→
                                                                      ```

                                                                        Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive report:

## PostgreSQL Migration Compatibility Analysis

### Summary
**Status**: HIGHLY COMPATIBLE - No critical blockers found. The codebase is well-structured for PostgreSQL migration with only minor considerations needed.

---

### Key Findings

#### 1. **Models & Column Types** ✅ FULLY COMPATIBLE
All SQLAlchemy column types used are database-agnostic:
- `Column(Integer, ...)` - Native in PostgreSQL
- `Column(String, ...)` - Native in PostgreSQL
- `Column(Text, ...)` - Native in PostgreSQL
- `Column(Float, ...)` - Native in PostgreSQL
- `Column(DateTime, ...)` - Native in PostgreSQL (uses Python timezone-aware datetime)
- `Column(JSON, ...)` - Native in PostgreSQL (SQLite JSON is text-based, PostgreSQL has native JSON/JSONB)

**Files**: `backend/app/models/matiere.py`, `document.py`, `flashcard.py`, `quiz.py`, `fiche.py`

---

#### 2. **Primary Keys & Autoincrement** ✅ COMPATIBLE
```python
id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
```
- SQLAlchemy handles autoincrement transparently
- PostgreSQL uses `SERIAL` or `IDENTITY` by default for `Integer PRIMARY KEY`
- No explicit `autoincrement=True` needed (SQLAlchemy 1.4+ behavior)
- **No migration changes required**

---

#### 3. **Foreign Keys & Relationships** ✅ COMPATIBLE
```python
matiere_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("matieres.id"), nullable=True)
fiche_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("fiches.id", ondelete="CASCADE"), nullable=False)
```
- Standard SQL foreign key constraints work identically in PostgreSQL
- `ondelete="CASCADE"` is supported in PostgreSQL
- Relationships with `back_populates` and `cascade` work across both databases

---

#### 4. **Random Ordering** ⚠️ MINOR CONSIDERATION
**Location**: `backend/app/routers/flashcards.py:83` and `quiz.py:112`
```python
.order_by(func.random())
```
- **SQLite**: Uses `RANDOM()` function
- **PostgreSQL**: Uses `RANDOM()` function (also available)
- **Status**: COMPATIBLE - Both databases support `RANDOM()`

---

#### 5. **Query Patterns** ✅ FULLY COMPATIBLE
All routers use SQLAlchemy ORM exclusively (no raw SQL):
- `select()` - Database-agnostic
- `.where()` - Database-agnostic
- `.order_by()` - Database-agnostic
- `.limit()` / `.offset()` - Database-agnostic
- `func.random()` - Supported in both
- `selectinload()` - Database-agnostic eager loading

**Verified across all routers**: `matiere.py`, `documents.py`, `flashcards.py`, `quiz.py`, `fiches.py`, `recommandations.py`, `vocal.py`

---

#### 6. **Database Configuration** ⚠️ URL CHANGE REQUIRED
**File**: `backend/app/config.py:11`
```python
database_url: str = f"sqlite:///{PROJECT_ROOT / 'data' / 'iaca.db'}"
```

**PostgreSQL migration requires**:
```python
# Environment variable or hardcoded:
database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/iaca_db"
```

**File**: `backend/app/database.py:6`
```python
DATABASE_URL = settings.database_url.replace("sqlite:///", "sqlite+aiosqlite:///")
```

**PostgreSQL version** (no aiosqlite needed):
```python
DATABASE_URL = settings.database_url  # asyncpg already built into URL scheme
```

---

#### 7. **JSON Column Handling** ℹ️ IMPROVEMENT OPPORTUNITY
**File**: `backend/app/models/quiz.py:27`
```python
choix = Column(JSON, nullable=False)  # ["A", "B", "C", "D"]
```

- **SQLite**: Stores as text, SQLAlchemy serializes/deserializes JSON automatically
- **PostgreSQL**: Native JSON type with better query performance
- **Status**: COMPATIBLE - SQLAlchemy handles serialization transparently
- **Recommendation**: No code changes needed; PostgreSQL will use native JSONB (more efficient)

---

#### 8. **DateTime Timezone Handling** ✅ COMPATIBLE
```python
created_at = Column(DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
prochaine_revision = Column(DateTime, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc), index=True)
```

- Both SQLite and PostgreSQL support timezone-aware datetimes
- Python defaults to UTC consistently
- **Status**: COMPATIBLE

---

### Database Migration Checklist

| Item | Status | Action |
|------|--------|--------|
| Column types | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| Primary keys | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| Foreign keys | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| Relationships | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| ORM queries | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| Random function | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| JSON columns | ✅ Compatible | None required (improvement: use JSONB) |
| DateTime timezone | ✅ Compatible | None required |
| **DATABASE_URL** | ⚠️ **Change needed** | Update `config.py` + `.env` |
| **AsyncSQL driver** | ⚠️ **Change needed** | Replace aiosqlite with asyncpg in `database.py` |
| Raw SQL queries | ✅ None found | No refactoring needed |

---

### Implementation Steps for PostgreSQL

1. **Update `.env`**:
   ```
   DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/iaca_db
   ```

2. **Update `backend/app/config.py`**:
   ```python
   database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://user:password@localhost:5432/iaca_db"
   ```

3. **Update `backend/app/database.py`**:
   ```python
   # Remove the SQLite-specific replacement line
   DATABASE_URL = settings.database_url  # asyncpg handles it directly
   ```

4. **Install PostgreSQL driver**:
   ```bash
   pip install asyncpg
   ```

5. **Run migrations** (Alembic recommended for production):
   ```bash
   alembic upgrade head
   ```

---

### Conclusion

**✅ NO CRITICAL ISSUES**: The codebase is **database-agnostic** by design. All SQLAlchemy models and routers use standard ORM patterns that work identically in PostgreSQL and SQLite.

**Migration effort**: ~15 minutes for configuration changes. Zero code refactoring needed in models or routers.

                                                                           1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→set -euo pipefail
     4→
     5→run_step() {
     6→    local step_label="$1"
     7→    shift
     8→
     9→    echo "[START] ${step_label}"
    10→    if "$@"; then
    11→        echo "[PASS]  ${step_label}"
    12→    else
    13→        local exit_code=$?
    14→        echo "[FAIL]  ${step_label} (exit ${exit_code})" >&2
    15→        exit "${exit_code}"
    16→    fi
    17→}
    18→
    19→run_step "1/4 backend compileall" backend/.venv/bin/python -m compileall backend/app
    20→run_step "2/4 security regression" bash scripts/test_security_regression.sh
    21→run_step "3/4 frontend typecheck" bash -lc 'cd frontend && ./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit'
    22→
    23→if [[ "${FAST:-0}" == "1" ]]; then
    24→    echo "[START] 4/4 frontend build"
    25→    echo "[PASS]  4/4 frontend build (skipped: FAST=1)"
    26→else
    27→    run_step "4/4 frontend build" bash -lc 'cd frontend && npm run build'
    28→fi
    29→
    30→echo "[PASS]  Ralph full validation completed"

                                                                          ```
                                                                               1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# =============================================================================
     3→# test_security_regression.sh — Tests de régression sécurité IACA backend
     4→# =============================================================================
     5→#
     6→# Usage:
     7→#   bash scripts/test_security_regression.sh
     8→#
     9→# Ce script :
    10→#   1) Démarre l'API FastAPI sur un port dynamique (8099+RANDOM) avec :
    11→#        API_AUTH_TOKEN=test-secret
    12→#        RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true
    13→#        RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS=30  (marge pour tests 1-4, burst test5=40 déclenche 429)
    14→#        RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS=60
    15→#   2) Teste POST /api/matieres sans token          → attend 401
    16→#   3) Teste POST /api/matieres avec token valide   → attend 201 ou 409
    17→#   4) Teste WS /api/vocal/ws sans token            → attend 1008 ou 403/InvalidStatus
    18→#   5) Teste WS /api/vocal/ws avec ?token=test-secret → attend connexion acceptée
    19→#   6) Burst de requêtes GET /api/vocal/status      → attend au moins un 429
    20→#   7) Arrête l'API et retourne exit 0 (tout OK) ou exit 1 (au moins un échec)
    21→#
    22→# Prérequis : curl, backend/.venv (uvicorn + websockets déjà installés)
    23→# =============================================================================
    24→
    25→set -euo pipefail
    26→
    27→# Dynamic port: 8100–8599 to avoid stale-server conflicts
    28→PORT=$(( 8100 + (RANDOM % 500) ))
    29→# Kill any stale process already on this port
    30→fuser -k "${PORT}/tcp" 2>/dev/null || true
    31→BASE_URL="http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}"
    32→TOKEN="test-secret"
    33→BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../backend" && pwd)"
    34→PYTHON="${BACKEND_DIR}/.venv/bin/python3"
    35→UVICORN="${BACKEND_DIR}/.venv/bin/uvicorn"
    36→LOG="/tmp/iaca_security_test_$$.log"
    37→SERVER_PID=""
    38→FAILURES=0
    39→
    40→# ── Colors ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    41→RED='\033[0;31m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; NC='\033[0m'
    42→pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}  PASS${NC} $1"; }
    43→fail() { echo -e "${RED}  FAIL${NC} $1"; FAILURES=$((FAILURES+1)); }
    44→info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}  ....${NC} $1"; }
    45→
    46→# ── Cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    47→cleanup() {
    48→    if [[ -n "$SERVER_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
    49→        info "Arrêt du serveur (PID $SERVER_PID)…"
    50→        kill "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
    51→        wait "$SERVER_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
    52→    fi
    53→    rm -f "$LOG"
    54→}
    55→trap cleanup EXIT
    56→
    57→# ── Start server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    58→echo "=== Démarrage API sur 127.0.0.1:${PORT} ==="
    59→(
    60→  cd "$BACKEND_DIR"
    61→  API_AUTH_TOKEN="$TOKEN" \
    62→  RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED="true" \
    63→  RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS="30" \
    64→  RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS="60" \
    65→  "$UVICORN" app.main:app \
    66→    --host 127.0.0.1 --port "$PORT" \
    67→    --log-level warning \
    68→    > "$LOG" 2>&1
    69→) &
    70→SERVER_PID=$!
    71→
    72→# Wait for server to be ready (up to 60s)
    73→info "Attente démarrage serveur…"
    74→for i in $(seq 1 120); do
    75→    if curl -sf "${BASE_URL}/health" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    76→        pass "Serveur prêt (tentative ${i})"
    77→        break
    78→    fi
    79→    sleep 0.5
    80→    if [[ $i -eq 120 ]]; then
    81→        echo "--- LOG SERVEUR ---"
    82→        cat "$LOG"
    83→        fail "Serveur non démarré après 60s"
    84→        exit 1
    85→    fi
    86→done
    87→
    88→echo ""
    89→echo "=== TEST 1: POST /api/matieres sans token → 401 ==="
    90→STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    91→    -X POST "${BASE_URL}/api/matieres" \
    92→    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    93→    -d '{"nom":"TestRegression","description":"regression"}')
    94→if [[ "$STATUS" == "401" ]]; then
    95→    pass "POST sans token → 401 (obtenu: $STATUS)"
    96→else
    97→    fail "POST sans token → attendu 401, obtenu $STATUS"
    98→fi
    99→
   100→echo ""
   101→echo "=== TEST 2: POST /api/matieres avec token valide → 201 ou 409 ==="
   102→STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
   103→    -X POST "${BASE_URL}/api/matieres" \
   104→    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   105→    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
   106→    -d '{"nom":"TestRegression","description":"regression"}')
   107→if [[ "$STATUS" == "201" || "$STATUS" == "409" ]]; then
   108→    pass "POST avec token → $STATUS (201 créé ou 409 déjà existant)"
   109→else
   110→    fail "POST avec token → attendu 201/409, obtenu $STATUS"
   111→fi
   112→
   113→echo ""
   114→echo "=== TEST 3: WebSocket sans token → close 1008 ==="
   115→WS_NO_TOKEN=$("$PYTHON" - <<PYEOF
   116→import asyncio, sys
   117→try:
   118→    import websockets
   119→except ImportError:
   120→    print("NO_WEBSOCKETS")
   121→    sys.exit(0)
   122→
   123→async def test():
   124→    try:
   125→        async with websockets.connect("ws://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/api/vocal/ws") as ws:
   126→            await ws.recv()
   127→            print("ACCEPTED")
   128→    except websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosedError as e:
   129→        code = e.rcvd.code if e.rcvd else 0
   130→        print(f"CLOSED_{code}")
   131→    except websockets.exceptions.InvalidStatus as e:
   132→        # websockets v16: HTTP rejection before upgrade (e.g. 403)
   133→        print(f"REJECTED_{e.response.status_code}")
   134→    except Exception as e:
   135→        print(f"ERROR:{type(e).__name__}:{e}")
   136→
   137→asyncio.run(test())
   138→PYEOF
   139→)
   140→if [[ "$WS_NO_TOKEN" == "CLOSED_1008" || "$WS_NO_TOKEN" == "REJECTED_HTTP" || "$WS_NO_TOKEN" =~ ^REJECTED_ ]]; then
   141→    pass "WS sans token → rejeté ($WS_NO_TOKEN)"
   142→elif [[ "$WS_NO_TOKEN" == "NO_WEBSOCKETS" ]]; then
   143→    info "SKIP WS tests — websockets non installé dans le venv"
   144→else
   145→    fail "WS sans token → attendu rejet (1008/403), obtenu $WS_NO_TOKEN"
   146→fi
   147→
   148→echo ""
   149→echo "=== TEST 4: WebSocket avec ?token=test-secret → connexion acceptée ==="
   150→WS_WITH_TOKEN=$("$PYTHON" - <<PYEOF
   151→import asyncio, sys
   152→try:
   153→    import websockets
   154→except ImportError:
   155→    print("NO_WEBSOCKETS")
   156→    sys.exit(0)
   157→
   158→async def test():
   159→    try:
   160→        async with websockets.connect(
   161→            "ws://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/api/vocal/ws?token=${TOKEN}",
   162→            open_timeout=5,
   163→        ) as ws:
   164→            await ws.send('{"type":"pong"}')
   165→            print("ACCEPTED")
   166→    except websockets.exceptions.ConnectionClosedError as e:
   167→        code = e.rcvd.code if e.rcvd else 0
   168→        print(f"CLOSED_{code}")
   169→    except Exception as e:
   170→        print(f"ERROR:{e}")
   171→
   172→asyncio.run(test())
   173→PYEOF
   174→)
   175→if [[ "$WS_WITH_TOKEN" == "ACCEPTED" ]]; then
   176→    pass "WS avec token → connexion acceptée"
   177→elif [[ "$WS_WITH_TOKEN" == "NO_WEBSOCKETS" ]]; then
   178→    info "SKIP — websockets non installé dans le venv"
   179→else
   180→    fail "WS avec token → attendu ACCEPTED, obtenu $WS_WITH_TOKEN"
   181→fi
   182→
   183→echo ""
   184→echo "=== TEST 5: Burst GET /api/vocal/status → au moins un 429 ==="
   185→# Rate limit: 30 req/60s. Burst 40 requests, expect ≥1 × 429.
   186→GOT_429=0
   187→for i in $(seq 1 40); do
   188→    S=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
   189→        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
   190→        "${BASE_URL}/api/vocal/status")
   191→    if [[ "$S" == "429" ]]; then
   192→        GOT_429=$((GOT_429+1))
   193→    fi
   194→done
   195→if [[ $GOT_429 -ge 1 ]]; then
   196→    pass "Burst → $GOT_429 réponse(s) 429 reçues"
   197→else
   198→    fail "Burst → aucun 429 reçu (rate-limit non déclenché ?)"
   199→fi
   200→
   201→# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   202→echo ""
   203→echo "==========================================="
   204→if [[ $FAILURES -eq 0 ]]; then
   205→    echo -e "${GREEN}TOUS LES TESTS PASSENT (0 échec)${NC}"
   206→    exit 0
   207→else
   208→    echo -e "${RED}${FAILURES} TEST(S) ÉCHOUÉ(S)${NC}"
   209→    exit 1
   210→fi
   211→
                                                                          ```

## Deployment Files Summary

I found **7 deployment-related files** in the IACA project. Here's what exists:

### Core Deployment Files

#### 1. **docker-compose.yml** (Root)
- **Status**: Configured and complete
- **Services**: 3 services (backend, frontend, ollama)
- **Key Features**:
  - Backend: FastAPI on port 8000, depends_on ollama (service_healthy condition)
  - Frontend: Next.js on port 3000, depends_on backend
  - Ollama: Latest image with healthcheck (ollama list test), 30s intervals, volume for persistence
  - OLLAMA_HOST correctly set to `http://ollama:11434` for container communication
  - Environment variables loaded from `.env` file
  - Volumes: ollama_data (named volume), frontend/backend code mounts, data/docs directories

#### 2. **backend/Dockerfile**
- **Status**: Production-ready
- **Base Image**: Python 3.11-slim
- **Setup**:
  - Installs ffmpeg (for audio processing)
  - Installs Python dependencies from requirements.txt
  - Exposes port 8000
  - Runs uvicorn with dynamic PORT environment variable
- **Note**: Minimal, efficient image

#### 3. **frontend/Dockerfile**
- **Status**: Production-ready
- **Base Image**: Node.js 20-alpine
- **Setup**:
  - npm ci (clean install)
  - Exposes port 3000
  - Runs `npm run dev` with hostname 0.0.0.0
- **Note**: Lightweight Alpine image, development server configured for Docker

#### 4. **vercel.json** (Root)
- **Status**: Configured
- **Framework**: Next.js
- **Build Command**: `npm run vercel-build` (defined in root package.json)
- **Output Directory**: `frontend/.next` (monorepo aware)
- **Install Command**: `npm install`
- **Schema**: OpenAPI-compliant

#### 5. **frontend/next.config.js**
- **Status**: API proxy configured
- **Key Features**:
  - API rewrites: `/api/:path*` → backend target
  - Smart target selection: prioritizes `INTERNAL_API_URL` (Docker/server-side), falls back to `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (client-side), defaults to `http://localhost:8000`
  - Helper function: `buildApiRewriteDestination()` handles trailing slashes
  - Environment-aware configuration

#### 6. **.env.example** (Root)
- **Status**: Complete with all deployment variables
- **Key Sections**:
  - **Application**: APP_NAME, DEBUG flag
  - **Database**: SQLite URL
  - **LLM APIs**: Anthropic API key, Google Gemini key
  - **Ollama**: OLLAMA_HOST (localhost for dev, `http://ollama:11434` for Docker)
  - **Security**: API_AUTH_TOKEN, rate limiting config (RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED, RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS, RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS)
  - **CORS**: localhost:3000 and 127.0.0.1:3000
  - **Speech**: Whisper model (base), Piper voice (French)
  - **Frontend**: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_AUTH_TOKEN, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, INTERNAL_API_URL
  - **Paths**: DOCS_PATH, UPLOAD_PATH

#### 7. **package.json** (Root)
- **Status**: Monorepo workspace configured
- **Workspaces**: frontend
- **Scripts**:
  - `build`: workspace build
  - `vercel-build`: explicit Vercel build (frontend only)
- **Dependencies**: Next 14.1.0, React 18.2.0, React-dom 18.2.0
- **Package Manager**: npm@10.9.2

### Validation/Deployment Scripts

#### **scripts/ralph_full_validation.sh**
- **Purpose**: Multi-stage deployment validation pipeline
- **4 Stages**:
  1. Backend Python compile check
  2. Security regression tests (auth, rate-limit, WebSocket)
  3. Frontend TypeScript type checking
  4. Frontend Next.js build (skipped if FAST=1)
- **Status**: PASS/FAIL exit codes, used in Ralph cycle

#### **scripts/test_security_regression.sh**
- **Purpose**: Comprehensive security testing for pre-deployment
- **5 Core Tests**:
  1. POST /api/matieres without token → expects 401
  2. POST /api/matieres with Bearer token → expects 201/409
  3. WebSocket /api/vocal/ws without token → expects close/rejection
  4. WebSocket /api/vocal/ws with ?token parameter → expects connection accepted
  5. Burst rate-limit test (40 requests) → expects ≥1 × 429
- **Configuration**: Dynamic port (8100-8599), configurable rate limits, cleanup trap
- **Status**: Actively maintained, part of validation pipeline

### Deployment Targets Configured

| Platform | Status | Config | Notes |
|----------|--------|--------|-------|
| **Vercel** | Ready | vercel.json | Frontend (Next.js) deployment |
| **Docker Compose** | Ready | docker-compose.yml | Local/self-hosted full stack |
| **Railway** (mentioned in CLAUDE.md) | Not configured | - | Backend target (no railway.json found) |
| **Render** | Not configured | - | No render.yaml |
| **Fly.io** | Not configured | - | No fly.toml |
| **Netlify** | Not configured | - | No netlify.toml |
| **Heroku** | Not configured | - | No Procfile |

### Critical Deployment Notes

1. **Monorepo Structure**: Root package.json with frontend workspace for Vercel compatibility
2. **Environment-aware**: Separate INTERNAL_API_URL (server-side) and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL (client-side)
3. **Security Built-in**: Auth token and rate-limiting configurable via environment
4. **Health Checks**: Ollama has container health probe; backend and frontend startup configurable
5. **Data Persistence**: Named volume `ollama_data` for Ollama models; data/ and docs/ directories mounted
6. **Next.js Output**: Default standalone output (configured implicitly in next.config.js via rewrites)

All deployment infrastructure is **present and functional** for Docker Compose and Vercel. Additional platforms (Railway, Render) are mentioned in CLAUDE.md Phase 0 but have no config files yet.

                         1→---
     2→name: universal-orchestrator
     3→description: Codex-led multi-LLM orchestrator. Use when coordinating multiple LLMs via tmux, distributing tasks with IDs, syncing CLAUDE.md, and running test/debug/fix loops with agent support.
     4→tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task
     5→model: codex
     6→permissionMode: dangerously-skip
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Universal Orchestrator v2026.2 (Codex Lead)
    10→
    11→You are the orchestrator. Your PRIMARY identity is coordinator and distributor.
    12→Codex leads by default. Claude gets the hardest tasks.
    13→Priority order: Claude (hardest), AMP, Codex, Antigravity (if available).
    14→
    15→You also do real work yourself; do not act as a pure dispatcher.
    16→
    17→**CRITICAL RULE: You are ALWAYS the orchestrator.** After any interruption, compaction, or context loss, your first action is to re-read CLAUDE.md and redistribute tasks within 30 seconds. Never do passive analysis. Act immediately.
    18→
    19→## Skills (load only when needed)
    20→
    21→- `@agents_library/agent-orchestrator-universal/skills/communication-inter-agents/SKILL.md`
    22→- `@agents_library/agent-orchestrator-universal/skills/task-distribution-memory-sync/SKILL.md`
    23→- `@agents_library/agent-orchestrator-universal/skills/quota-monitoring-handoff/SKILL.md` (optional, mainly for quota-limited orchestrators)
    24→- `@agents_library/agent-orchestrator-universal/skills/session-skill-harvester/SKILL.md`
    25→
    26→## Mission
    27→
    28→- Coordinate multiple LLMs in parallel via tmux.
    29→- Decompose work into **short, precise, atomic tasks** (15-30 min max per task).
    30→- Distribute tasks by priority order and context.
    31→- Keep `CLAUDE.md` as the single source of truth — **every action gets logged**.
    32→- Apply a test/debug/fix loop after implementation.
    33→- Use agents from `agents_library` whenever a relevant agent or skill exists.
    34→- Operate autonomously: only ask the user when a true blocker exists.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Session Bootstrap Protocol (MANDATORY at session start)
    39→
    40→Every time you start or resume a session, execute this checklist **before doing anything else**:
    41→
    42→1. **Read CLAUDE.md** — understand current state, in-progress tasks, blocked items.
    43→2. **Confirm tmux session** — `tmux list-windows -t $SESSION` to verify all workers.
    44→3. **Check worker health** — `tmux capture-pane -t $SESSION:N -p | tail -5` for each window.
    45→4. **Identify gaps** — tasks marked IN_PROGRESS with no activity = stale. Re-assign them.
    46→5. **Load skills** — load communication-inter-agents and task-distribution-memory-sync.
    47→6. **Distribute immediately** — if there are PENDING tasks, send them to idle workers NOW.
    48→7. **Log session start** in CLAUDE.md: timestamp, orchestrator identity, worker status.
    49→
    50→**Time budget: < 60 seconds for the entire bootstrap.** Do not analyze; act.
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→## Post-Compaction Recovery Protocol (MANDATORY)
    55→
    56→When context is compacted or you lose continuity:
    57→
    58→1. **Re-read CLAUDE.md immediately** — this is your memory. Trust it.
    59→2. **Identify idle workers** — any worker without an IN_PROGRESS task needs a task NOW.
    60→3. **Redistribute within 30 seconds** — do not spend time "understanding the situation."
    61→4. **Do not re-analyze completed work** — if CLAUDE.md says COMPLETED, trust it and move on.
    62→5. **Log the recovery** in CLAUDE.md: `| <timestamp> | RECOVERY | Orchestrator resumed after compaction |`
    63→
    64→**The #1 failure mode is going passive after compaction.** Fight this instinct. Your job is to distribute, not to contemplate.
    65→
    66→---
    67→
    68→## Golden Rules
    69→
    70→1. Do not code before: LLM healthcheck, quick code scan, and docs check (Context7 if available).
    71→2. **Always update CLAUDE.md before AND after task assignments.** Every task change = CLAUDE.md update.
    72→3. Never queue more than 2 tasks per worker.
    73→4. Verify prompt submission after each tmux send (use `send-verified.sh`).
    74→   - If no activity is visible, send Enter again; if still idle, send `C-c` and re-send.
    75→   - **Always double-Enter** after multi-line tmux send-keys to Claude Code workers.
    76→5. Do not skip tests after implementation.
    77→6. Only report to the user after the test phase is complete (unless blocked).
    78→7. Never stop between batches. If idle, sleep 60-90s, poll, and continue.
    79→8. Ask questions only when blocked. Do not pause for unnecessary confirmations.
    80→9. Every task must specify a relevant agent from `agents_library` (or explicitly note none).
    81→10. **One agent per shared resource.** Never have 2+ workers doing the same download, install, or DB migration.
    82→11. **Monitor long operations actively** — poll downloads/builds/installs every 2 minutes.
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→## Task Decomposition Rules
    87→
    88→Tasks MUST be:
    89→
    90→- **Atomic**: one clear objective, one deliverable.
    91→- **Short**: 15-30 minutes max. If it takes longer, split it.
    92→- **Precise**: exact files to touch, exact endpoint/function/component to create or fix.
    93→- **Testable**: clear acceptance criteria — how do you know it's done?
    94→- **Self-contained**: all context needed is in the prompt. Worker should not need to ask questions.
    95→
    96→### Bad task examples (TOO VAGUE):
    97→- "Fix the backend" — which file? which bug? what's the expected behavior?
    98→- "Test the API" — which endpoints? what are the expected responses?
    99→- "Set up the frontend" — which components? what should they do?
   100→
   101→### Good task examples (PRECISE):
   102→- "Fix HTTP 500 in POST /api/flashcards/ — the Pydantic schema FlashcardBase is missing the `matiere_id` field. Add it as Optional[int]. Test: POST with matiere_id=1 returns 201."
   103→- "Create GET /api/matieres/{id}/stats endpoint — returns {nb_documents, nb_flashcards, nb_quiz}. File: routers/matiere.py. Test: GET /api/matieres/1/stats returns JSON with 3 keys."
   104→- "Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in frontend/ and fix any type errors. Acceptance: exit code 0."
   105→
   106→---
   107→
   108→## Quickstart
   109→
   110→1) Ensure `agents_library/` is available inside the project root.
   111→2) Ensure `CLAUDE.md` exists (template: `@agents_library/agent-orchestrator-universal/skills/task-distribution-memory-sync/assets/claude-md-template.md`).
   112→3) Start your tmux session (or attach to an existing one).
   113→4) Confirm window numbers with `tmux list-windows -t $SESSION`.
   114→5) **Run the Session Bootstrap Protocol.**
   115→
   116→## Shared Memory: CLAUDE.md
   117→
   118→`CLAUDE.md` is the shared source of truth. Keep it concise and current.
   119→Minimum sections to maintain:
   120→- Global state (goal, progress, orchestrator, session notes)
   121→- Task Assignment Queue (ID, task, agent, assignee, priority, status, acceptance criteria)
   122→- Task Completion Log (timestamp, LLM, task ID, result summary)
   123→- Inter-LLM Messages
   124→- Remaining or blocked tasks
   125→
   126→### CLAUDE.md Update Discipline
   127→
   128→- **Before distributing**: write the task row with status PENDING.
   129→- **After sending prompt**: update status to IN_PROGRESS + note the target LLM/window.
   130→- **On completion**: update status to COMPLETED + add completion log entry with details.
   131→- **On failure**: update status to BLOCKED + add reason + reassign if possible.
   132→- **Every 5 minutes minimum**: re-read CLAUDE.md to detect worker updates.
   133→
   134→## Role Allocation (default)
   135→
   136→Priority order for assignment:
   137→1) Claude: hardest tasks, critical architecture, risky changes.
   138→2) AMP: large implementation and multi-file work.
   139→3) Codex: integration, glue code, smaller fixes, tests, final review.
   140→4) Antigravity (if available): deep reasoning, tradeoffs, tricky bugs.
   141→
   142→Adjust based on availability and context. Keep 2 tasks max per worker.
   143→
   144→---
   145→
   146→## Orchestration Loop (always-on)
   147→
   148→### 0) Preflight
   149→   - Confirm session and windows.
   150→   - Read `CLAUDE.md` and identify current state.
   151→   - Load relevant skills from `agents_library` as needed.
   152→   - If any required information is missing, create a BLOCKED task and ask the user once.
   153→
   154→### 1) Decompose
   155→   - Break the request into **short, atomic tasks** (15-30 min each).
   156→   - Assign IDs `T-001`, `T-002`, ...
   157→   - For each task, write:
   158→     - **What**: precise action to take.
   159→     - **Where**: exact files/endpoints/components.
   160→     - **Acceptance**: how to verify it's done (test command, expected output).
   161→     - **Agent**: which `agents_library` agent to use.
   162→   - Write all tasks to `CLAUDE.md` with status `PENDING`.
   163→
   164→### 2) Distribute
   165→   - Send one task per prompt using tmux.
   166→   - Each prompt MUST include: task ID, precise description, acceptance criteria, agent to load, and CLAUDE.md update instructions.
   167→   - Verify submission via `send-verified.sh` or `tmux capture-pane`.
   168→   - **Double-Enter** for multi-line prompts to Claude Code workers.
   169→
   170→### 3) Work in Parallel
   171→   - While workers run, the orchestrator completes assigned tasks in the repo.
   172→   - Use specialized agents from `agents_library` when available.
   173→
   174→### 4) Poll and Redistribute
   175→   - Poll `CLAUDE.md` every 60-90 seconds.
   176→   - Reassign tasks immediately when a worker finishes.
   177→   - If a task is blocked, mark it and re-route or unblock.
   178→   - **Monitor long operations** (downloads, builds) every 2 minutes.
   179→   - If nothing is pending, sleep 60-90 seconds and continue. Do not stop.
   180→
   181→### 5) Test Phase
   182→   - Create test tasks for each completed feature.
   183→   - Run Ralph cycles (test -> debug -> fix) until pass or blocked.
   184→   - Log results in `CLAUDE.md`.
   185→
   186→### 6) Final Report (only after all tasks and tests are done)
   187→   - Summarize features, tests, and any remaining blockers.
   188→   - Update progress to 100% in `CLAUDE.md`.
   189→
   190→### 7) Skill Harvesting (post-session)
   191→   - If tasks were long or debug-heavy, run the session skill harvester.
   192→   - Generate reusable skills and register them in `CLAUDE.md`.
   193→
   194→---
   195→
   196→## Resource Coordination Rules
   197→
   198→Shared resources (downloads, DB migrations, installs) must be coordinated:
   199→
   200→1. **One agent per resource.** Before starting a download/install, check if another worker is already doing it.
   201→2. **Claim before acting.** Write in CLAUDE.md: `RESOURCE_LOCK: <resource> claimed by <worker>`.
   202→3. **Release after completion.** Update the lock entry on completion or failure.
   203→4. **Poll external processes.** For downloads > 1 min, poll every 2 min: `ollama list`, `pip list`, `ls -la <file>`.
   204→5. **Kill duplicates immediately.** If you detect duplicate processes for the same resource, kill all but one.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## Task Prompt Template
   209→
   210→Use a **precise, self-contained** prompt:
   211→
   212→```
   213→Task T-XXX: <one-line summary>
   214→
   215→What: <precise description of what to do>
   216→Files: <exact files to create or modify>
   217→Acceptance: <how to verify — test command and expected output>
   218→
   219→Load agent: @agents_library/<agent-name>.md
   220→
   221→When done:
   222→1) Update CLAUDE.md Task Assignment Queue: T-XXX -> COMPLETED
   223→2) Add a Task Completion Log entry: timestamp, files touched, test result, notes
   224→```
   225→
   226→### Example prompt:
   227→
   228→```
   229→Task T-012: Fix FlashcardBase schema missing matiere_id field
   230→
   231→What: Add `matiere_id: Optional[int] = None` to FlashcardBase in backend/app/schemas/flashcard.py
   232→Files: backend/app/schemas/flashcard.py
   233→Acceptance: POST /api/flashcards/ with {"question":"test","reponse":"test","matiere_id":1} returns 201
   234→
   235→Load agent: @agents_library/backend-architect.md
   236→
   237→When done:
   238→1) Update CLAUDE.md Task Assignment Queue: T-012 -> COMPLETED
   239→2) Add a Task Completion Log entry: timestamp, files touched, test result, notes
   240→```
   241→
   242→---
   243→
   244→## Ralph Method (short)
   245→
   246→1) Test
   247→2) If failed, debug
   248→3) Apply fix
   249→4) Repeat (max 3 cycles) then mark blocked
   250→
   251→## Quota / Handoff (optional)
   252→
   253→If the orchestrator is quota-limited, load:
   254→`@agents_library/agent-orchestrator-universal/skills/quota-monitoring-handoff/SKILL.md`
   255→
   256→## Tmux Rules
   257→
   258→- Use numeric window IDs when possible.
   259→- `Enter` must be a real keypress (not quoted).
   260→- **Always double-Enter** for multi-line prompts to Claude Code workers.
   261→- Always verify with `tmux capture-pane` after sending.
   262→
   263→```bash
   264→# Send a task
   265→tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:N "Task T-001: ... (see template)" Enter
   266→
   267→# IMPORTANT: double-Enter for Claude Code (multi-line paste eats first Enter)
   268→tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:N Enter
   269→
   270→# Verify execution
   271→tmux capture-pane -t $SESSION:N -p | tail -15
   272→
   273→# Resend Enter if the prompt was not submitted
   274→tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:N Enter
   275→```
   276→
   277→---
   278→
   279→## Anti-Patterns (from post-mortem analysis)
   280→
   281→These are known failure modes. **Actively avoid them:**
   282→
   283→| Anti-Pattern | What happens | Fix |
   284→|---|---|---|
   285→| Passive post-compaction | Orchestrator reads and analyzes instead of distributing | Re-read CLAUDE.md + distribute in < 30s |
   286→| Vague tasks | Workers ask questions or produce wrong output | Use task decomposition rules (precise, testable) |
   287→| Concurrent shared resource | Multiple workers download/install the same thing | One agent per resource, claim in CLAUDE.md |
   288→| No monitoring of long ops | Download fails silently, pipeline stays blocked | Poll every 2 min |
   289→| CLAUDE.md stale | Workers don't know what others are doing | Update before AND after every assignment |
   290→| Enter swallowed by paste | Prompt visible but not submitted in Claude Code | Always double-Enter + verify with capture-pane |
   291→| Over-trusting COMPLETED | Not testing after completion | Always run acceptance test before marking done |
   292→
   293→---
   294→
   295→Universal Orchestrator v2026.2 is ready. Await the next user request.
   296→
                    ```

                        ## 👤 user (12:00:26)

                        ✅ **Result:**
                        ```
                             1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# start-orchestrator.sh
     3→# Lance 3 sessions Claude (haiku, sonnet, opus) + autres LLMs
     4→
     5→SESSION="iaca-orchestration"
     6→PROJECT="/home/julien/Documents/IACA"
     7→LLM_STARTUP_WAIT=5
     8→
     9→echo "🚀 Démarrage Universal Orchestrator (Multi-Claude)..."
    10→echo "   Projet: $PROJECT"
    11→
    12→# Vérifier si session existe déjà
    13→if tmux has-session -t $SESSION 2>/dev/null; then
    14→    echo "⚠️  Session $SESSION existe déjà."
    15→    read -p "Supprimer et recréer ? (y/n) " -n 1 -r
    16→    echo
    17→    if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    18→        tmux kill-session -t $SESSION
    19→    else
    20→        echo "Attachement à la session existante..."
    21→        tmux attach -t $SESSION
    22→        exit 0
    23→    fi
    24→fi
    25→
    26→# Créer session avec fenêtre principale
    27→echo "📦 Création session tmux..."
    28→tmux new-session -d -s $SESSION -n main -c $PROJECT
    29→
    30→# Créer fenêtres pour chaque LLM
    31→echo "📦 Création des fenêtres..."
    32→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n claude-haiku -c $PROJECT       # w2
    33→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n claude-sonnet -c $PROJECT      # w3
    34→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n claude-opus -c $PROJECT        # w4
    35→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n amp -c $PROJECT                # w5
    36→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n codex -c $PROJECT              # w6
    37→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n antigravity-proxy -c $PROJECT  # w7
    38→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n antigravity -c $PROJECT        # w8
    39→tmux new-window -t $SESSION -n ollama -c $PROJECT             # w9
    40→
    41→# Démarrer les 3 Claudes avec --dangerously-skip-permissions
    42→echo "🤖 Démarrage Claude Haiku (w2 - tâches simples H*)..."
    43→tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:2 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model haiku" Enter
    44→sleep $LLM_STARTUP_WAIT
    45→
    46→echo "🤖 Démarrage Claude Sonnet (w3 - tâches moyennes S*)..."
    47→tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:3 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model sonnet" Enter
    48→sleep $LLM_STARTUP_WAIT
    49→
    50→echo "🤖 Démarrage Claude Opus (w4 - tâches complexes O*)..."
    51→tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:4 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus" Enter
    52→sleep $LLM_STARTUP_WAIT
    53→
    54→# Démarrer Amp si disponible
    55→if command -v amp &> /dev/null; then
    56→    echo "🤖 Démarrage Amp (w5 - backup complexe)..."
    57→    tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:5 "amp -m large --dangerously-allow-all" Enter
    58→    sleep $LLM_STARTUP_WAIT
    59→else
    60→    echo "⏭️  Amp non installé"
    61→fi
    62→
    63→# Démarrer Codex si disponible
    64→if command -v codex &> /dev/null; then
    65→    echo "🤖 Démarrage Codex (w6 - backup simple)..."
    66→    tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:6 "codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox" Enter
    67→    sleep $LLM_STARTUP_WAIT
    68→else
    69→    echo "⏭️  Codex non installé"
    70→fi
    71→
    72→# Antigravity si disponible
    73→if command -v antigravity-claude-proxy &> /dev/null; then
    74→    echo "🔌 Démarrage Antigravity Proxy (w7)..."
    75→    tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:7 "antigravity-claude-proxy start" Enter

    79→    tmux send-keys -t $SESSION:8 "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-opus-4-5-thinking" Enter
    80→else

    82→fi
    83→
    84→echo ""
    85→echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
    86→echo "✅ Universal Orchestrator prêt !"

                            ## 👤 user (12:00:27)

                            ✅ **Result:**
                            ```
                                 1→# RAPPORT D'ORCHESTRATION IACA
     2→
     3→> Date : 2026-02-27
     4→> Orchestrateur : Claude Opus 4.6 (window 4 - amp)
     5→> Session tmux : iaca-orchestration
     6→> Plan executé : PLAN_INFRA.md (33 tâches : H1-H8, S1-S14, O1-O11)
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## 1. CONTEXTE
    11→
    12→### Objectif initial
    13→
    14→Exécuter le PLAN_INFRA.md via un système d'orchestration multi-LLM :
    15→- **3 sessions Claude Code** dans des fenêtres tmux séparées
    16→- Distribution par difficulté : H* → Haiku (cheap), S* → Sonnet (moyen), O* → Opus (complexe)
    17→- Orchestrateur Opus qui dispatche, poll et reboucle
    18→- Économie de tokens en utilisant le bon modèle pour chaque tâche
    19→
    20→### État de la session tmux au lancement
    21→
    22→| Window | Nom | Modèle détecté | État |
    23→|--------|-----|----------------|------|
    24→| 0 | main | - | Terminal shell |
    25→| 1 | claude-haiku | Aucun | Shell vide (nvm error) |
    26→| 2 | claude-sonnet | **Haiku 4.5** | Claude Code prêt, prompt vide |
    27→| 3 | claude-opus | **Sonnet 4.6** | Claude Code prêt, prompt vide |
    28→| 4 | amp | **Opus 4.6** | Orchestrateur (moi) |
    29→| 5 | codex | - | Non utilisé |
    30→| 8 | ollama | - | Non utilisé |
    31→
    32→**Note :** Les modèles étaient mal assignés aux fenêtres (Haiku dans "sonnet", Sonnet dans "opus"), mais cela n'a pas eu d'impact car l'orchestration tmux n'a finalement pas fonctionné.
    33→
    34→### État du projet au lancement
    35→
    36→- Aucun dossier `backend/` ni `frontend/`
    37→- Projet vierge (uniquement docs, plans, configs agent)
    38→- Pas de CLAUDE.md de tracking
    39→- Documents source : 1128 fichiers dans le dossier Téléchargements
    40→
    41→---
    42→
    43→## 2. TENTATIVE D'ORCHESTRATION TMUX
    44→
    45→### 2.1. Envoi de la première tâche
    46→
    47→**Action :** Envoi du batch H1-H8 à Haiku (window 2) via :
    48→```bash
    49→tmux send-keys -t iaca-orchestration:2 "Lis PLAN_INFRA.md et execute les taches H1 a H8..." Enter
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Résultat :** Le texte est arrivé dans Claude Code mais en mode "paste detection" :
    53→```
    54→❯ [Pasted text #1 +1 lines]
    55→```
    56→Claude Code attendait une confirmation pour le texte collé.
    57→
    58→### 2.2. Confirmation du paste
    59→
    60→**Action :** Envoi d'un Enter supplémentaire :
    61→```bash
    62→tmux send-keys -t iaca-orchestration:2 Enter
    63→```
    64→
    65→**Résultat :** Haiku a commencé à travailler :
    66→- Lu PLAN_INFRA.md ✓
    67→- Analysé les tâches ✓
    68→- Lancé la première commande bash (mkdir) ✓
    69→
    70→### 2.3. Blocage sur les permissions
    71→
    72→**Problème critique :** Claude Code a affiché un prompt de permission interactif :
    73→
    74→```
    75→Bash command
    76→  mkdir -p backend/app/{models,routers,services,schemas} && touch backend/app/__init__.py
    77→  Command contains brace expansion that could alter command parsing
    78→Do you want to proceed?
    79→❯ 1. Yes
    80→  2. No
    81→```
    82→
    83→**Action :** Envoi de "1" + Enter pour approuver :
    84→```bash
    85→tmux send-keys -t iaca-orchestration:2 "1" Enter
    86→```
    87→
    88→**Résultat :** La commande a été approuvée, mais le problème se répèterait pour chaque outil suivant (Write, Edit, Bash, etc.). Une tâche H* simple nécessite ~5-10 appels d'outils, donc 5-10 approbations manuelles.
    89→
    90→### 2.4. Pourquoi c'est non viable
    91→
    92→| Problème | Impact |
    93→|----------|--------|
    94→| **Chaque outil demande confirmation** | 33 tâches × ~8 outils = ~264 approbations à envoyer via tmux |
    95→| **Pas de détection fiable du prompt** | `tmux capture-pane` retourne du texte brut avec codes ANSI, difficile à parser pour détecter "Do you want to proceed?" |
    96→| **Timing imprévisible** | Le délai entre l'envoi et le prompt de permission varie (réseau, complexité, modèle) |
    97→| **Pas de feedback structuré** | Impossible de savoir si la commande a réussi ou échoué sans parser l'output visuel |
    98→| **Race conditions** | Si j'envoie "1" avant que le prompt apparaisse, ça tape "1" dans le prompt de saisie de Claude Code |
    99→
   100→### 2.5. Décision
   101→
   102→J'ai présenté 3 options à l'utilisateur :
   103→1. **Je fais tout moi-même** (recommandé) — pas de blocage
   104→2. **Activer auto-approve** — user va dans chaque fenêtre régler les permissions
   105→3. **Approbation manuelle** — user approuve dans chaque fenêtre pendant que je poll
   106→
   107→**Choix de l'utilisateur : Option 1 — tout faire moi-même.**
   108→
