                                        ```
                                             1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Import v3: Dynamic bulk import from Drive using matiere_mapping.json.
     4→Creates matieres via API if missing, uploads files with dedup (title check).
     5→Usage: python3 scripts/import_docs_v3.py [drive_root]
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import os
     9→import sys
    10→import json
    11→import time
    12→import io
    13→import urllib.request
    14→import urllib.error
    15→
    16→SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
    17→MAPPING_FILE = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "matiere_mapping.json")
    18→BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")
    19→DRIVE_ROOT = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else os.path.expanduser(
    20→    "~/Téléchargements/drive-download-20260226T072425Z-3-001"
    21→)
    22→LOG_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(SCRIPT_DIR), "logs")
    23→LOG_FILE = os.path.join(LOG_DIR, "import_v3.log")
    24→
    25→ALLOWED_EXT = {".pdf", ".pptx", ".docx"}
    26→
    27→
    28→def load_mapping():
    29→    """Load matiere_mapping.json."""
    30→    with open(MAPPING_FILE, "r") as f:
    31→        return json.load(f)
    32→
    33→
    34→def api_get(path):
    35→    """GET request to API."""
    36→    try:
    37→        req = urllib.request.Request(f"{BASE_URL}{path}")
    38→        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
    39→            return json.loads(resp.read())
    40→    except Exception as e:
    41→        print(f"  [ERR] GET {path}: {e}")
    42→        return None
    43→
    44→
    45→def api_post_json(path, data):
    46→    """POST JSON to API."""
    47→    body = json.dumps(data).encode()
    48→    req = urllib.request.Request(
    49→        f"{BASE_URL}{path}",
    50→        data=body,
    51→        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
    52→        method="POST",
    53→    )
    54→    try:
    55→        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:
    56→            return resp.status, json.loads(resp.read())
    57→    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    58→        return e.code, e.read().decode()
    59→    except Exception as e:
    60→        return 0, str(e)
    61→
    62→
    63→def multipart_encode(fields, files):
    64→    """Encode multipart/form-data."""
    65→    boundary = b"----PythonImportV3Boundary7369421"
    66→    body = io.BytesIO()
    67→    for name, value in fields.items():
    68→        body.write(b"--" + boundary + b"\r\n")
    69→        body.write(f'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="{name}"\r\n\r\n'.encode())
    70→        body.write(str(value).encode() + b"\r\n")
    71→    for name, (filename, data, content_type) in files.items():
    72→        body.write(b"--" + boundary + b"\r\n")
    73→        body.write(f'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="{name}"; filename="{filename}"\r\n'.encode())
    74→        body.write(f"Content-Type: {content_type}\r\n\r\n".encode())
    75→        body.write(data + b"\r\n")
    76→    body.write(b"--" + boundary + b"--\r\n")
    77→    return body.getvalue(), b"multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary
    78→
    79→
    80→def upload_file(file_path, matiere_id, chapitre, tags):
    81→    """Upload a file via POST /api/documents/upload."""
    82→    filename = os.path.basename(file_path)
    83→    ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1].lower()
    84→    content_type = {
    85→        ".pdf": "application/pdf",
    86→        ".docx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document",
    87→        ".pptx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation",
    88→    }.get(ext, "application/octet-stream")
    89→
    90→    with open(file_path, "rb") as f:
    91→        file_data = f.read()
    92→
    93→    fields = {
    94→        "matiere_id": str(matiere_id),
    95→        "chapitre": chapitre,
    96→        "tags": tags,
    97→    }
    98→    files = {"file": (filename, file_data, content_type)}
    99→    body, content_type_header = multipart_encode(fields, files)
   100→
   101→    req = urllib.request.Request(
   102→        f"{BASE_URL}/api/documents/upload",
   103→        data=body,
   104→        headers={"Content-Type": content_type_header.decode()},
   105→        method="POST",
   106→    )
   107→    try:
   108→        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as resp:
   109→            return resp.status, json.loads(resp.read())
   110→    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
   111→        return e.code, e.read().decode()
   112→    except Exception as e:
   113→        return 0, str(e)
   114→
   115→
   116→def get_or_create_matiere(mapping_entry, existing_matieres):
   117→    """Find matiere by name or create it via API. Returns matiere_id."""
   118→    name = mapping_entry["matiere"]
   119→
   120→    # Check existing
   121→    for m in existing_matieres:
   122→        if m["nom"].lower().strip() == name.lower().strip():
   123→            return m["id"], False
   124→
   125→    # Create new
   126→    status, resp = api_post_json("/api/matieres/", {
   127→        "nom": name,
   128→        "description": mapping_entry.get("description", ""),
   129→        "couleur": mapping_entry.get("couleur", "#2563eb"),
   130→    })
   131→    if 200 <= status < 300:
   132→        existing_matieres.append(resp)
   133→        return resp["id"], True
   134→    else:
   135→        print(f"  [ERR] Creation matiere '{name}' echouee: HTTP {status}")
   136→        return None, False
   137→
   138→
   139→def get_existing_titles():
   140→    """Get set of existing document titles for dedup."""
   141→    docs = api_get("/api/documents/")
   142→    if docs is None:
   143→        return set()
   144→    return {d.get("titre", "").lower().strip() for d in docs}
   145→
   146→
   147→def collect_files(mapping, drive_root):
   148→    """Collect all files to import based on mapping."""
   149→    files = []
   150→    mappings = mapping["mappings"]
   151→
   152→    for entry in mappings:
   153→        rel_path = entry["path"]
   154→        full_dir = os.path.join(drive_root, rel_path)
   155→        if not os.path.isdir(full_dir):
   156→            print(f"[WARN] Dossier manquant: {full_dir}")
   157→            continue
   158→
   159→        for root, _, filenames in os.walk(full_dir):
   160→            for fname in sorted(filenames):
   161→                ext = os.path.splitext(fname)[1].lower()
   162→                if ext not in ALLOWED_EXT:
   163→                    continue
   164→                full_path = os.path.join(root, fname)
   165→                relative = os.path.relpath(full_path, full_dir)
   166→                chapitre = os.path.dirname(relative)
   167→                if chapitre == ".":
   168→                    chapitre = ""
   169→                files.append({
   170→                    "path": full_path,
   171→                    "mapping_entry": entry,
   172→                    "chapitre": chapitre,
   173→                    "tags": entry["matiere"],
   174→                })
   175→
   176→    # Root files
   177→    root_matiere = mapping.get("root_files_matiere", "Documents divers")
   178→    for fname in sorted(os.listdir(drive_root)):
   179→        full_path = os.path.join(drive_root, fname)
   180→        if not os.path.isfile(full_path):
   181→            continue
   182→        ext = os.path.splitext(fname)[1].lower()
   183→        if ext not in ALLOWED_EXT:
   184→            continue
   185→        files.append({
   186→            "path": full_path,
   187→            "mapping_entry": {
   188→                "matiere": root_matiere,
   189→                "description": mapping.get("root_files_description", ""),
   190→                "couleur": mapping.get("root_files_couleur", "#64748b"),
   191→            },
   192→            "chapitre": "",
   193→            "tags": root_matiere,
   194→        })
   195→
   196→    return files
   197→
   198→
   199→def main():
   200→    os.makedirs(LOG_DIR, exist_ok=True)
   201→    print(f"Import v3 - Drive: {DRIVE_ROOT}")
   202→    print(f"API: {BASE_URL}")
   203→    print(f"Mapping: {MAPPING_FILE}")
   204→    print("-" * 60)
   205→
   206→    # Load mapping
   207→    mapping = load_mapping()
   208→    print(f"Mappings: {len(mapping['mappings'])} dossiers configures")
   209→
   210→    # Get existing matieres
   211→    existing_matieres = api_get("/api/matieres/") or []
   212→    print(f"Matieres existantes: {len(existing_matieres)}")
   213→
   214→    # Get existing titles for dedup
   215→    existing_titles = get_existing_titles()
   216→    print(f"Documents existants: {len(existing_titles)} (pour dedup)")
   217→
   218→    # Collect files
   219→    files = collect_files(mapping, DRIVE_ROOT)
   220→    print(f"Fichiers a traiter: {len(files)}")
   221→    print("=" * 60)
   222→
   223→    # Resolve matiere IDs
   224→    matiere_cache = {}
   225→    for entry in mapping["mappings"]:
   226→        name = entry["matiere"]
   227→        if name not in matiere_cache:
   228→            mid, created = get_or_create_matiere(entry, existing_matieres)
   229→            matiere_cache[name] = mid
   230→            if created:
   231→                print(f"  [NEW] Matiere creee: {name} (id={mid})")
   232→
   233→    # Root files matiere
   234→    root_name = mapping.get("root_files_matiere", "Documents divers")
   235→    if root_name not in matiere_cache:
   236→        mid, created = get_or_create_matiere({
   237→            "matiere": root_name,
   238→            "description": mapping.get("root_files_description", ""),
   239→            "couleur": mapping.get("root_files_couleur", "#64748b"),
   240→        }, existing_matieres)
   241→        matiere_cache[root_name] = mid
   242→        if created:
   243→            print(f"  [NEW] Matiere creee: {root_name} (id={mid})")
   244→
   245→    print(f"\nMatieres resolues: {len(matiere_cache)}")
   246→    print("-" * 60)
   247→
   248→    # Upload
   249→    uploaded = 0
   250→    skipped = 0
   251→    failed = 0
   252→    total = len(files)
   253→
   254→    with open(LOG_FILE, "w") as log:
   255→        log.write(f"Import v3 demarre: {time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n")
   256→        log.write(f"Drive: {DRIVE_ROOT}\n")
   257→        log.write(f"Total fichiers: {total}\n\n")
   258→
   259→        for i, f in enumerate(files, 1):
   260→            path = f["path"]
   261→            fname = os.path.basename(path)
   262→            title_key = os.path.splitext(fname)[0].lower().strip()
   263→            matiere_name = f["mapping_entry"]["matiere"]
   264→            matiere_id = matiere_cache.get(matiere_name)
   265→
   266→            if not matiere_id:
   267→                failed += 1
   268→                log.write(f"[KO] {path} - matiere '{matiere_name}' non resolue\n")
   269→                print(f"[{i:3d}/{total}] SKIP (matiere?) {fname[:50]}")
   270→                continue
   271→
   272→            # Dedup check
   273→            if title_key in existing_titles:
   274→                skipped += 1
   275→                log.write(f"[SKIP] {path} - titre deja en DB\n")
   276→                if i <= 20 or i % 50 == 0:
   277→                    print(f"[{i:3d}/{total}] SKIP (dedup) {fname[:50]}")
   278→                continue
   279→
   280→            size_mb = os.path.getsize(path) / 1024 / 1024
   281→            print(f"[{i:3d}/{total}] {matiere_name[:20]:20s} {fname[:40]} ({size_mb:.1f}MB)...", end=" ", flush=True)
   282→
   283→            status, resp = upload_file(path, matiere_id, f["chapitre"], f["tags"])
   284→
   285→            if 200 <= status < 300:
   286→                uploaded += 1
   287→                doc_id = resp.get("id", "?") if isinstance(resp, dict) else "?"
   288→                existing_titles.add(title_key)
   289→                print(f"OK (id={doc_id})")
   290→                log.write(f"[OK] {path} -> doc_id={doc_id}, matiere={matiere_name}\n")
   291→            else:
   292→                failed += 1
   293→                err = resp if isinstance(resp, str) else json.dumps(resp)[:100]
   294→                print(f"ERR HTTP {status}: {err[:60]}")
   295→                log.write(f"[KO] {path} HTTP {status}: {err}\n")
   296→
   297→        log.write(f"\nImport termine: {time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}\n")
   298→        log.write(f"Resultat: {uploaded} OK, {skipped} skipped, {failed} KO\n")
   299→
   300→    print("=" * 60)
   301→    print(f"Resultat: {uploaded} uploades, {skipped} dedupliques, {failed} erreurs")
   302→    print(f"Log: {LOG_FILE}")
   303→
   304→    # Summary per matiere
   305→    print("\nRepartition matieres:")
   306→    for name, mid in sorted(matiere_cache.items()):
   307→        print(f"  {name}: id={mid}")
   308→
   309→
   310→if __name__ == "__main__":
   311→    main()
   312→
                                        ```

                       1→from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
     2→from sqlalchemy import select
     3→from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
     4→
     5→from app.database import get_db
     6→from app.models.document import Document
     7→from app.models.flashcard import Flashcard
     8→from app.models.matiere import Matiere
     9→from app.models.quiz import Quiz, QuizQuestion
    10→from app.models.fiche import Fiche, FicheSection
    11→from app.services import claude_service, gemini_service, document_parser
    12→
    13→router = APIRouter()
    14→EXTRACTION_ERROR_PREFIX = "[Erreur d'extraction:"
    15→MIN_EXTRACTED_CONTENT_CHARS = 120
    16→
    17→
    18→def _contains_extraction_error_marker(content: str | None) -> bool:
    19→    return EXTRACTION_ERROR_PREFIX in (content or "")
    20→
    21→
    22→async def _resolve_document_content(doc: Document, db: AsyncSession) -> str:
    23→    cached_content = (doc.contenu_extrait or "").strip()
    24→    if cached_content and not _contains_extraction_error_marker(cached_content):
    25→        if len(cached_content) >= MIN_EXTRACTED_CONTENT_CHARS:
    26→            return cached_content
    27→
    28→    try:
    29→        extracted_content = (await document_parser.parse_document(doc.fichier_path)).strip()
    30→    except Exception as exc:
    31→        raise HTTPException(
    32→            status_code=422,
    33→            detail=f"Extraction impossible pour ce document: {exc}",
    34→        ) from exc
    35→
    36→    if (
    37→        not extracted_content
    38→        or _contains_extraction_error_marker(extracted_content)
    39→        or len(extracted_content) < MIN_EXTRACTED_CONTENT_CHARS
    40→    ):
    41→        raise HTTPException(
    42→            status_code=422,
    43→            detail="Le document ne contient pas assez de contenu exploitable pour la generation.",
    44→        )
    45→
    46→    doc.contenu_extrait = extracted_content
    47→    await db.commit()
    48→    return extracted_content
    49→
    50→
    51→@router.post("/generer-flashcards/{document_id}")
    52→async def pipeline_flashcards(
    53→    document_id: int,
    54→    nb: int = 10,
    55→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    56→):
    57→    """O10: Pipeline complet - Document → extraction → Claude → flashcards en DB."""
    58→    result = await db.execute(select(Document).where(Document.id == document_id))
    59→    doc = result.scalar_one_or_none()
    60→    if not doc:
    61→        raise HTTPException(404, "Document non trouvé")
    62→
    63→    contenu = await _resolve_document_content(doc, db)
    64→
    65→    # Get matiere name
    66→    matiere_nom = "droit public"
    67→    if doc.matiere_id:
    68→        mat_result = await db.execute(select(Matiere).where(Matiere.id == doc.matiere_id))
    69→        mat = mat_result.scalar_one_or_none()
    70→        if mat:
    71→            matiere_nom = mat.nom
    72→
    73→    # Generate flashcards via Claude
    74→    try:
    75→        flashcards_data = await claude_service.generer_flashcards(contenu, matiere_nom, nb)
    76→    except ValueError as exc:
    77→        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc)) from exc
    78→
    79→    # Save to DB
    80→    created = []
    81→    for fc in flashcards_data:
    82→        flashcard = Flashcard(
    83→            question=fc["question"],
    84→            reponse=fc["reponse"],
    85→            explication=fc.get("explication", ""),
    86→            difficulte=fc.get("difficulte", 1),
    87→            matiere_id=doc.matiere_id,
    88→            document_id=doc.id,
    89→        )
    90→        db.add(flashcard)
    91→        created.append(fc)
    92→
    93→    await db.commit()
    94→    return {"generated": len(created), "flashcards": created}
    95→
    96→
    97→@router.post("/generer-qcm/{document_id}")
    98→async def pipeline_qcm(
    99→    document_id: int,
   100→    nb: int = 5,
   101→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   102→):
   103→    """Pipeline: Document → Claude → QCM en DB."""
   104→    result = await db.execute(select(Document).where(Document.id == document_id))
   105→    doc = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   106→    if not doc:
   107→        raise HTTPException(404, "Document non trouvé")
   108→
   109→    contenu = await _resolve_document_content(doc, db)
   110→
   111→    matiere_nom = "droit public"
   112→    if doc.matiere_id:
   113→        mat_result = await db.execute(select(Matiere).where(Matiere.id == doc.matiere_id))
   114→        mat = mat_result.scalar_one_or_none()
   115→        if mat:
   116→            matiere_nom = mat.nom
   117→
   118→    try:
   119→        qcm_data = await claude_service.generer_qcm(contenu, matiere_nom, nb)
   120→    except ValueError as exc:
   121→        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc)) from exc
   122→
   123→    quiz = Quiz(
   124→        titre=f"QCM - {doc.titre}",
   125→        matiere_id=doc.matiere_id,
   126→        document_id=doc.id,
   127→    )
   128→    db.add(quiz)
   129→    await db.flush()
   130→
   131→    for q in qcm_data:
   132→        question = QuizQuestion(
   133→            quiz_id=quiz.id,
   134→            question=q["question"],
   135→            choix=q["choix"],
   136→            reponse_correcte=q["reponse_correcte"],
   137→            explication=q.get("explication", ""),
   138→            difficulte=q.get("difficulte", 1),
   139→        )
   140→        db.add(question)
   141→
   142→    await db.commit()
   143→    return {"quiz_id": quiz.id, "questions_generated": len(qcm_data)}
   144→
   145→
   146→@router.post("/generer-fiche/{document_id}")
   147→async def pipeline_fiche(
   148→    document_id: int,
   149→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   150→):
   151→    """Pipeline: Document -> Claude -> Fiche de revision structuree en DB."""
   152→    result = await db.execute(select(Document).where(Document.id == document_id))
   153→    doc = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   154→    if not doc:
   155→        raise HTTPException(404, "Document non trouve")
   156→
   157→    contenu = await _resolve_document_content(doc, db)
   158→
   159→    matiere_nom = "droit public"
   160→    if doc.matiere_id:
   161→        mat_result = await db.execute(select(Matiere).where(Matiere.id == doc.matiere_id))
   162→        mat = mat_result.scalar_one_or_none()
   163→        if mat:
   164→            matiere_nom = mat.nom
   165→
   166→    try:
   167→        fiche_data = await claude_service.generer_fiche(contenu, matiere_nom, doc.titre)
   168→    except ValueError as exc:
   169→        raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail=str(exc)) from exc
   170→
   171→    fiche = Fiche(
   172→        titre=fiche_data.get("titre", f"Fiche - {doc.titre}"),
   173→        resume=fiche_data.get("resume", ""),
   174→        matiere_id=doc.matiere_id,
   175→        document_id=doc.id,
   176→        tags=matiere_nom,
   177→    )
   178→    db.add(fiche)
   179→    await db.flush()
   180→
   181→    sections = fiche_data.get("sections", [])
   182→    inserted_sections = 0
   183→    for i, s in enumerate(sections):
   184→        sec_titre = s.get("titre", f"Section {i+1}")
   185→        sec_contenu = s.get("contenu", "")
   186→        if len(str(sec_contenu).strip()) < 40:
   187→            continue
   188→        section = FicheSection(
   189→            fiche_id=fiche.id,
   190→            titre=sec_titre,
   191→            contenu=sec_contenu,
   192→            ordre=inserted_sections,
   193→        )
   194→        db.add(section)
   195→        inserted_sections += 1
   196→
   197→    await db.commit()
   198→    return {
   199→        "fiche_id": fiche.id,
   200→        "titre": fiche.titre,
   201→        "sections_generated": inserted_sections,
   202→    }
   203→
   204→
   205→@router.get("/ressources/{matiere_id}")
   206→async def pipeline_recommandations(
   207→    matiere_id: int,
   208→    sujet: str = "",
   209→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   210→):
   211→    """O11: Pipeline recommandations externes via Gemini."""
   212→    result = await db.execute(select(Matiere).where(Matiere.id == matiere_id))
   213→    matiere = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   214→    if not matiere:
   215→        raise HTTPException(404, "Matière non trouvée")
   216→
   217→    query = sujet if sujet else matiere.nom
   218→    ressources = await gemini_service.rechercher_ressources(query, matiere.nom)
   219→    return ressources
   220→
   221→
   222→@router.get("/mindmap/{document_id}")
   223→async def generer_mind_map(
   224→    document_id: int,
   225→    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
   226→):
   227→    """Generate a mind map from document content via Gemini."""
   228→    result = await db.execute(select(Document).where(Document.id == document_id))
   229→    doc = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   230→    if not doc:
   231→        raise HTTPException(404, "Document non trouvé")
   232→
   233→    contenu = await _resolve_document_content(doc, db)
   234→
   235→    try:
   236→        mindmap = await gemini_service.generer_mind_map(contenu, doc.titre)
   237→        return mindmap
   238→    except Exception as exc:
   239→        raise HTTPException(
   240→            status_code=503,
   241→            detail=f"Generation mind map indisponible: {exc}",
   242→        ) from exc
   243→
                  ```

Services**: Claude CLI (flashcards/QCM/fiches), Ollama qwen2:0.5b (vocal), Piper TTS, Whisper STT\n- **Git**: main branch, `.gitignore` excludes `data/`, `docs/`, `.env`\n\n## DB Volumes (as of P19)\n\n- 1165 documents, 21 matieres\n- 26996 flashcards, 5856 quizzes (12907 questions), 2699 fiches (12488 sections)\n\n## Architecture\n\n```\nbackend/app/          FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, SQLite\n  routers/            quiz, flashcards, fiches, matieres, documents, vocal, recommandations\n  services/           ollama_service (RAG + streaming), claude_service, whisper_service, piper_service\n  models/             document, flashcard, quiz, fiche (+fiche_section), matiere\n  middleware/          rate_limit.py, security.py (Bearer auth)\n  main.py             lifespan pattern, CORS localhost:3000\n\nfrontend/app/         Next.js 14 App Router, Tailwind\n  page.tsx            Dashboard (5 stat cards, quick actions)\n  quiz/page.tsx       Quiz with multi-matiere interleaving\n  flashcards/page.tsx Flashcards grouped by matiere, SM-2, filters\n  fiches/page.tsx     Fiches filterable, accordion detail\n  vocal/page.tsx      Real-time streaming vocal (text_chunk/audio_chunk WS)\n  matieres/page.tsx   Matiere list with counters\n\nfrontend/components/  FlashCard, QuizQuestion, VocalChat (streaming + model selector), MindMap\nfrontend/lib/auth.ts  withAuthHeaders(), withAuthQuery() for optional Bearer token\n\nscripts/              generate_full_coverage.py, fix_generic_flashcards.py, ensure_min_quizzes_per_matiere.py,\n                      audit_content_quality.py, upgrade_manuals_quality.py, ralph_full_validation.sh,\n                      test_security_regression.sh, import_docs_v3.py, dedup_documents.py\n```\n\n## Key API Patterns\n\n- Auth: optional Bearer token via `API_AUTH_TOKEN` env var (middleware skips GET by default)\n- Rate limiting: configurable via `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED`, `RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS`, `RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS`\n- Pagination: `limit`/`offset` on quiz, flashcards, fiches endpoints\n- Interleaving: list endpoints distribute results across matieres (not biased to one)\n- Vocal WS: `ws://localhost:8000/api/vocal/ws?model=qwen2:0.5b` with streaming text_chunk/audio_chunk\n- AI generation: POST `/api/recommandations/generer-flashcards/{doc_id}`, `/generer-qcm/{doc_id}`, `/generer-fiche/{doc_id}`\n\n## Known Issues\n\n- Ollama mistral too large for this machine -> use qwen2:0.5b (or phi3:mini)\n- Backend must start with CLAUDE* env vars unset for Claude CLI subprocess to work\n- Frontend needs full restart after next.config.js proxy changes\n- tmux send-keys requires double-Enter (paste swallows first Enter)\n- Frontend `.next` cache can corrupt -> purge `.next` and restart on chunk 404 errors\n\n## User Preferences\n\n- French language for all documentation and UI\n- Orchestrator role expected (multi-LLM tmux windows)\n- Track all task progress in context compaction: redistribute tasks within 30s\n- One agent per download task\n- Always double-Enter after tmux send-keys\n- Tasks must be atomic, 15-30 min max, with clear acceptance criteria\n-                                                                                       ```
                                                                                           1→import asyncio
     2→import json
     3→import os
     4→import re
     5→from typing import Any
     6→
     7→
     8→FICHE_MIN_SECTIONS = 6
     9→FICHE_MAX_SECTIONS = 10
    10→FICHE_MIN_SECTION_CHARS = 220
    11→FICHE_MAX_SECTION_CHARS = 2400
    12→FICHE_MIN_RESUME_CHARS = 220
    13→
    14→GENERIC_SECTION_PATTERNS = (
    15→    r"^section\s*\d*$",
    16→    r"^partie\s*\d*$",
    17→    r"^chapitre\s*\d*$",
    18→    r"^introduction$",
    19→    r"^conclusion$",
    20→    r"^divers$",
    21→    r"^synthese$",
    22→)
    23→
    24→ACTIONABLE_MARKERS = (
    25→    "point d'application",
    26→    "exemple",
    27→    "methode",
    28→    "méthode",
    29→    "etape",
    30→    "étape",
    31→    "condition",
    32→    "exception",
    33→    "jurisprudence",
    34→    "article",
    35→    "procedure",
    36→    "procédure",
    37→)
    38→EXTRACTION_ERROR_PREFIX = "[Erreur d'extraction:"
    39→MIN_SOURCE_CONTENT_CHARS = 120
    40→
    41→
    42→async def run_claude_cli(prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 4096) -> str:
    43→    """Execute Claude via CLI (uses the user's Claude subscription)."""
    44→    env = os.environ.copy()
    45→    for key in list(env.keys()):
    46→        if key.startswith("CLAUDE"):
    47→            del env[key]
    48→    cmd = ["claude", "--print", "--output-format", "text"]
    49→    process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
    50→        *cmd,
    51→        stdin=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
    52→        stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
    53→        stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
    54→        env=env,
    55→    )
    56→    stdout, stderr = await process.communicate(input=prompt.encode())
    57→    if process.returncode != 0:
    58→        raise RuntimeError(f"Claude CLI error: {stderr.decode()}")
    59→    return stdout.decode().strip()
    60→
    61→
    62→def _normalize_text(value: str) -> str:
    63→    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", value or "").strip()
    64→
    65→
    66→def _sanitize_source_content(contenu: str) -> str:
    67→    lines = []
    68→    for raw_line in (contenu or "").replace("\r", "").splitlines():
    69→        if EXTRACTION_ERROR_PREFIX in raw_line:
    70→            continue
    71→        lines.append(raw_line)
    72→    return _normalize_text("\n".join(lines))
    73→
    74→
    75→def _ensure_generation_source(contenu: str) -> str:
    76→    source = _sanitize_source_content(contenu)
    77→    if len(source) < MIN_SOURCE_CONTENT_CHARS:
    78→        raise ValueError("Contenu source insuffisant ou invalide pour la generation.")
    79→    return source
    80→
    81→
    82→def _strip_code_fences(value: str) -> str:
    83→    text = value.strip()
    84→    if text.startswith("```"):
    85→        text = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    86→        text = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", text)
    87→    return text.strip()
    88→
    89→
    90→def _balanced_json_candidates(text: str, open_char: str, close_char: str) -> list[str]:
    91→    candidates: list[str] = []
    92→    depth = 0
    93→    start_index = -1
    94→
    95→    for index, char in enumerate(text):
    96→        if char == open_char:
    97→            if depth == 0:
    98→                start_index = index
    99→            depth += 1
   100→        elif char == close_char and depth > 0:
   101→            depth -= 1
   102→            if depth == 0 and start_index >= 0:
   103→                candidates.append(text[start_index:index + 1])
   104→                start_index = -1
   105→
   106→    # Try larger candidates first to keep full payload if available.
   107→    return sorted(candidates, key=len, reverse=True)
   108→
   109→
   110→def _extract_json_array(raw_output: str) -> list[Any]:
   111→    text = _strip_code_fences(raw_output)
   112→    candidates = [text] + _balanced_json_candidates(text, "[", "]")
   113→    for candidate in candidates:
   114→        try:
   115→            parsed = json.loads(candidate)
   116→            if isinstance(parsed, list):
   117→                return parsed
   118→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   119→            continue
   120→    raise ValueError("Claude n'a pas retourné un JSON array valide")
   121→
   122→
   123→def _extract_json_object(raw_output: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
   124→    text = _strip_code_fences(raw_output)
   125→    candidates = [text] + _balanced_json_candidates(text, "{", "}")
   126→    for candidate in candidates:
   127→        try:
   128→            parsed = json.loads(candidate)
   129→            if isinstance(parsed, dict):
   130→                return parsed
   131→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   132→            continue
   133→    raise ValueError("Claude n'a pas retourné un JSON object valide")
   134→
   135→
   136→def _is_generic_section_title(title: str) -> bool:
   137→    lowered = _normalize_text(title).lower()
   138→    if not lowered:
   139→        return True
   140→    return any(re.match(pattern, lowered) for pattern in GENERIC_SECTION_PATTERNS)
   141→
   142→
   143→def _contains_actionable_marker(content: str) -> bool:
   144→    lowered = content.lower()
   145→    return any(marker in lowered for marker in ACTIONABLE_MARKERS)
   146→
   147→
   148→def _derive_title_from_content(content: str, index: int) -> str:
   149→    words = re.findall(r"[A-Za-zÀ-ÿ0-9][A-Za-zÀ-ÿ0-9'’\-]+", content)
   150→    phrase = " ".join(words[:8]).strip()
   151→    if len(phrase) < 12:
   152→        return f"Axe clé {index + 1}"
   153→    return phrase[0].upper() + phrase[1:90]
   154→
   155→
   156→def _split_source_blocks(contenu: str) -> list[str]:
   157→    if not contenu:
   158→        return []
   159→
   160→    raw_blocks = re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", contenu)
   161→    blocks = [_normalize_text(block) for block in raw_blocks]
   162→    blocks = [block for block in blocks if len(block) >= 120]
   163→
   164→    if blocks:
   165→        return blocks
   166→
   167→    fallback_text = _normalize_text(contenu)
   168→    if not fallback_text:
   169→        return []
   170→
   171→    chunk_size = 900
   172→    return [fallback_text[i:i + chunk_size] for i in range(0, len(fallback_text), chunk_size)]
   173→
   174→
   175→def _build_fallback_sections(
   176→    contenu: str,
   177→    needed: int,
   178→    used_titles: set[str],
   179→    start_index: int,
   180→) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
   181→    sections: list[dict[str, str]] = []
   182→    for block in _split_source_blocks(contenu):
   183→        if len(sections) >= needed:
   184→            break
   185→
   186→        content = _normalize_text(block)[:FICHE_MAX_SECTION_CHARS]
   187→        if len(content) < FICHE_MIN_SECTION_CHARS:
   188→            continue
   189→
   190→        section_index = start_index + len(sections)
   191→        title = _derive_title_from_content(content, section_index)
   192→        if _is_generic_section_title(title) or title.lower() in used_titles:
   193→            title = f"Axe clé {section_index + 1}"
   194→
   195→        if not _contains_actionable_marker(content):
   196→            content = (
   197→                f"{content}\n\n"
   198→                "Point d'application: identifiez la règle utile, ses conditions de mise en oeuvre "
   199→                "et un cas pratique où l'appliquer."
   200→            )
   201→
   202→        used_titles.add(title.lower())
   203→        sections.append({"titre": title, "contenu": content})
   204→
   205→    return sections
   206→
   207→
   208→def _sanitize_sections(raw_sections: Any, contenu: str) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
   209→    sanitized: list[dict[str, str]] = []
   210→    used_titles: set[str] = set()
   211→
   212→    if isinstance(raw_sections, list):
   213→        source_sections = raw_sections
   214→    else:
   215→        source_sections = []
   216→
   217→    for raw_section in source_sections:
   218→        if not isinstance(raw_section, dict):
   219→            continue
   220→
   221→        title = _normalize_text(str(raw_section.get("titre", "")))
   222→        content = _normalize_text(str(raw_section.get("contenu", "")))
   223→
   224→        if not content:
   225→            continue
   226→        if _is_generic_section_title(title):
   227→            title = _derive_title_from_content(content, len(sanitized))
   228→
   229→        content = content[:FICHE_MAX_SECTION_CHARS]
   230→        if len(content) < FICHE_MIN_SECTION_CHARS:
   231→            continue
   232→        if title.lower() in used_titles:
   233→            continue
   234→        if not _contains_actionable_marker(content) and len(content) < 320:
   235→            continue
   236→
   237→        used_titles.add(title.lower())
   238→        sanitized.append({"titre": title, "contenu": content})
   239→
   240→        if len(sanitized) >= FICHE_MAX_SECTIONS:
   241→            break
   242→
   243→    if len(sanitized) < FICHE_MIN_SECTIONS:
   244→        needed = FICHE_MIN_SECTIONS - len(sanitized)
   245→        sanitized.extend(_build_fallback_sections(contenu, needed, used_titles, len(sanitized)))
   246→
   247→    return sanitized[:FICHE_MAX_SECTIONS]
   248→
   249→
   250→def _build_resume(raw_resume: Any, sections: list[dict[str, str]], contenu: str) -> str:
   251→    resume = _normalize_text(str(raw_resume or ""))
   252→    if len(resume) >= FICHE_MIN_RESUME_CHARS:
   253→        return resume[:1400]
   254→
   255→    if sections:
   256→        merged = " ".join(section["contenu"] for section in sections[:2])
   257→        resume = _normalize_text(merged)
   258→
   259→    if len(resume) < FICHE_MIN_RESUME_CHARS:
   260→        resume = _normalize_text(contenu)[:1400]
   261→
   262→    return resume[:1400]
   263→
   264→
   265→def _sanitize_fiche_payload(payload: Any, contenu: str, titre_doc: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
   266→    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
   267→        payload = {}
   268→
   269→    title = _normalize_text(str(payload.get("titre", "")))
   270→    if len(title) < 8:
   271→        title = f"Fiche - {titre_doc}"
   272→
   273→    sections = _sanitize_sections(payload.get("sections", []), contenu)
   274→    if not sections:
   275→        sections = _build_fallback_sections(contenu, FICHE_MIN_SECTIONS, set(), 0)
   276→
   277→    resume = _build_resume(payload.get("resume", ""), sections, contenu)
   278→
   279→    return {
   280→        "titre": title[:180],
   281→        "resume": resume,
   282→        "sections": sections,
   283→    }
   284→
   285→
   286→async def generer_flashcards(contenu: str, matiere: str, nb: int = 10) -> list[dict]:
   287→    """Generate flashcards from document content using Claude."""
   288→    source = _ensure_generation_source(contenu)
   289→    prompt = f"""Tu es un expert en {matiere} pour la préparation aux concours administratifs français.
   290→
   291→À partir du contenu suivant, génère exactement {nb} flashcards de révision.
   292→
   293→CONTENU:
   294→{source[:8000]}
   295→
   296→Réponds UNIQUEMENT en JSON valide, sans texte avant ou après:
   297→[
   298→  {{
   299→    "question": "...",
   300→    "reponse": "...",
   301→    "explication": "...",
   302→    "difficulte": 1
   303→  }}
   304→]
   305→
   306→Difficulté de 1 (basique) à 5 (expert). Les questions doivent tester la compréhension, pas juste la mémorisation."""
   307→
   308→    result = await run_claude_cli(prompt)
   309→    parsed = _extract_json_array(result)
   310→    return [item for item in parsed if isinstance(item, dict)]
   311→
   312→
   313→async def generer_qcm(contenu: str, matiere: str, nb: int = 5) -> list[dict]:
   314→    """Generate QCM questions from document content using Claude."""
   315→    source = _ensure_generation_source(contenu)
   316→    prompt = f"""Tu es un expert en {matiere} pour la préparation aux concours administratifs français.
   317→
   318→À partir du contenu suivant, génère exactement {nb} questions à choix multiples.
   319→
   320→CONTENU:
   321→{source[:8000]}
   322→
   323→Réponds UNIQUEMENT en JSON valide, sans texte avant ou après:
   324→[
   325→  {{
   326→    "question": "...",
   327→    "choix": ["A. ...", "B. ...", "C. ...", "D. ..."],
   328→    "reponse_correcte": 0,
   329→    "explication": "...",
   330→    "difficulte": 1
   331→  }}
   332→]
   333→
   334→reponse_correcte = index (0-3) du bon choix. Difficulté de 1 à 5.
   335→Inclus des cas pratiques juridiques quand pertinent."""
   336→
   337→    result = await run_claude_cli(prompt)
   338→    parsed = _extract_json_array(result)
   339→    return [item for item in parsed if isinstance(item, dict)]
   340→
   341→
   342→async def generer_fiche(contenu: str, matiere: str, titre_doc: str) -> dict:
   343→    """Generate a structured revision sheet from document content using Claude."""
   344→    source = _ensure_generation_source(contenu)
   345→    prompt = f"""Tu es un redacteur pedagogique expert en {matiere} pour les concours administratifs francais.
   346→
   347→Objectif: generer une fiche de revision de haute precision, exploitable sans ambiguite.
   348→
   349→DOCUMENT: {titre_doc}
   350→
   351→CONTENU SOURCE:
   352→{source[:8000]}
   353→
   354→Reponds UNIQUEMENT en JSON valide, sans markdown, sans texte avant/apres:
   355→{{
   356→  "titre": "Titre explicite de la fiche",
   357→  "resume": "Resume operationnel en 140 a 240 mots",
   358→  "sections": [
   359→    {{
   360→      "titre": "Titre specifique (pas generique)",
   361→      "contenu": "120 a 260 mots incluant definitions, conditions, exceptions, methode, exemple ou point d'application"
   362→    }}
   363→  ]
   364→}}
   365→
   366→Contraintes obligatoires:
   367→- 8 a 10 sections distinctes.
   368→- Couvrir les grands themes du document (pas uniquement un angle).
   369→- Interdits: sections vides, titres vagues (ex: Introduction, Divers, Section 1), phrases creuses.
   370→- Chaque section doit contenir au moins un element actionnable (exemple, methode, point d'application, erreur frequente).
   371→- Style clair, concret, pedagogique."""
   372→
   373→    result = await run_claude_cli(prompt)
   374→    payload = _extract_json_object(result)
   375→    return _sanitize_fiche_payload(payload, source, titre_doc)
   376→
   377→
   378→async def analyser_document(contenu: str) -> dict:
   379→    """Analyze a document to extract key concepts, summary, tags."""
   380→    source = _ensure_generation_source(contenu)
   381→    prompt = f"""Analyse ce document académique et extrais les informations structurées.
   382→
   383→CONTENU:
   384→{source[:6000]}
   385→
   386→Réponds UNIQUEMENT en JSON valide:
   387→{{
   388→  "resume": "résumé en 3-5 phrases",
   389→  "concepts_cles": ["concept1", "concept2", ...],
   390→  "tags": ["tag1", "tag2", ...],
   391→  "matiere_suggeree": "nom de la matière",
   392→  "difficulte_estimee": 3
   393→}}"""
   394→
   395→    result = await run_claude_cli(prompt)
   396→    return _extract_json_object(result)
   397→
                                                                                      ```

                                                                                                                  Here is a complete summary of all findings:

---

## 1. Library Availability

| Library | System Python3 | Backend venv (`backend/.venv`) |
|---------|---------------|-------------------------------|
| **pdfplumber** | NOT INSTALLED | NOT INSTALLED |
| **python-docx** | NOT INSTALLED | INSTALLED (v1.2.0) |

You will need to install `pdfplumber` (both system and/or venv depending on where scripts run). `python-docx` is already available in the venv.

---

## 2. Scripts in `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/scripts/`

Key extraction/generation scripts:
- `generate_full_coverage.py` (43 KB) -- main bulk generation pipeline (heuristic/claude/ollama providers, targets per doc, anti-dedup, checkpoint)
- `upgrade_manuals_quality.py` (39 KB) -- quality improvement pipeline for manuals
- `generate_content_batch.py` / `generate_priority_content.py` -- earlier batch generators
- `import_docs_v2.py` / `import_docs_v3.py` -- document import scripts (v3 uses `matiere_mapping.json`)
- `fix_generic_flashcards.py` -- cleans up generic "Consolidation ..." flashcards
- `ensure_min_quizzes_per_matiere.py` -- ensures minimum quiz count per matiere
- `audit_content_quality.py` -- quality audit (flags critical/high/medium/low issues)
- `clean_fiches.py` / `dedup_documents.py` -- cleanup utilities
- `ralph_full_validation.sh` / `test_security_regression.sh` -- validation scripts

---

## 3. Current Generation Approach (`claude_service.py`)

File: `/home/julien/Documents/IACA/backend/app/services/claude_service.py`

- Uses **Claude CLI** (`claude --print --output-format text`) invoked as a subprocess, stripping all `CLAUDE*` env vars first.
- Three generation functions:
  - `generer_flashcards(contenu, matiere, nb)` -- produces JSON array of `{question, reponse, explication, difficulte}`
  - `generer_qcm(contenu, matiere, nb)` -- produces JSON array of `{question, choix, reponse_correcte, explication, difficulte}`
  - `generer_fiche(contenu, matiere, titre_doc)` -- produces JSON object `{titre, resume, sections[{titre, contenu}]}`
- Input is **`contenu`** (extracted text from `documents.contenu_extrait`), truncated to 8000 chars.
- Robust JSON parsing with balanced bracket extraction and code fence stripping.
- Fiche generation has extensive sanitization: min 6 / max 10 sections, min 220 chars per section, anti-generic-title checks, actionable marker requirements, fallback section builder from raw content.

---

## 4. Database Models

| Model | Table | Key Columns |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| **Document** | `documents` | `id, titre, fichier_path, type_fichier, contenu_extrait (Text), matiere_id, chapitre, tags` |
| **Flashcard** | `flashcards` | `id, question, reponse, explication, difficulte (1-5), matiere_id, document_id, SM-2 fields (intervalle_jours, facteur_facilite, repetitions, prochaine_revision)` |
| **Quiz** | `quizzes` | `id, titre, matiere_id, document_id` |
| **QuizQuestion** | `quiz_questions` | `id, quiz_id (FK), question, choix (JSON), reponse_correcte (int index), explication, difficulte` |
| **Fiche** | `fiches` | `id, titre, resume, matiere_id, document_id, chapitre, tags, ordre` |
| **FicheSection** | `fiche_sections` | `id, fiche_id (FK CASCADE), titre, contenu, ordre` |

---

## 5. Database Current State

**22 matieres** (IDs 1-22):
| ID | Nom |
|----|-----|
| 1 | Droit public |
| 2 | Economie et finances publiques |
| 3 | Questions contemporaines |
| 4 | Questions sociales |
| 5 | Relations internationales |
| 6 | Economie |
| 7 | Finances publiques |
| 8 | Espagnol |
| 9-13 | Licence 2/3 semesters + fiches revision |
| 14 | TEDS (L3) |
| 15 | Scolarite L3 |
| 16 | Livres et manuels |
| 17-19 | M1 specialties (Droit/legistique, Politique eco, Management/egalites) |
| 20 | Documents divers |
| 21-22 | Test matieres (security regression) |

**Content volumes:**
| Table | Count |
|-------|-------|
| `documents` | **1,223** |
| `flashcards` | **27,264** |
| `quizzes` | **5,947** |
| `quiz_questions` | **13,127** |
| `fiches` | **1,261** |

---

## Key Takeaway for Pipeline

The current pipeline reads `documents.contenu_extrait` (pre-extracted text stored in DB) and sends it to Claude CLI. The `contenu_extrait` field is populated at import time. If you need to re-extract or improve extraction from the actual PDF/DOCX files, you will need to:
1. **Install `pdfplumber`** (`pip install pdfplumber` in the venv)
2. Use the existing `python-docx` (already in venv) for DOCX files
3. The actual files are referenced by `documents.fichier_path`

    nom = c.execute('SELECT nom FROM matieres WHERE id=?',(mid,)).fetchone()
    if nom:
        fc = c.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fiches WHERE matiere_id=?',(mid,)).fetchone()[0]
        fl = c.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM flashcards WHERE matiere_id=?',(mid,)).fetchone()[0]
        print(f'{nom[0]}: {fc} fiches, {fl} flashcards')
"

                      ## 👤 user (10:27:55)

                      ✅ **Result:**
                      ```
                           1→import axios from "axios"
     2→import { Poppler } from "node-poppler"
     3→import fs from "fs"
     4→import { getPresignedUrlForUpload } from "./file"
     5→import { logger } from "./logger"
     6→
     7→function getPDFPageImagePath(baseFilename: string, pageNum: number) {
     8→    const patterns = [
     9→        `${baseFilename}-${pageNum}.png`,
    10→        `${baseFilename}-0${pageNum}.png`
    11→    ];
    12→
    13→    for (const pattern of patterns) {
    14→        if (fs.existsSync(pattern)) {
    15→            return pattern
    16→        }
    17→    }
    18→
    19→    throw new Error(`Could not find page ${pageNum} with any known filename pattern`);
    20→}
    21→
    22→
    23→export async function convertPdfToImages(path: string, filename: string): Promise<Array<string>> {
    24→
    25→    const images: Array<string> = []
    26→
    27→    const res = await axios.get(path, { responseType: "arraybuffer" })
    28→
    29→    const poppler = new Poppler()
    30→
    31→    const info = await poppler.pdfInfo(res.data)
    32→
    33→    let infoObj: any = {}
    34→
    35→    for (const line of info.toString().split("\n")) {
    36→        const lineArr = line.split(":")
    37→        infoObj[lineArr[0].toString().trim()] = lineArr[1].toString().trim()
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    let pages: number = 0
    41→    pages = parseInt(infoObj.Pages)
    42→
    43→    for (let i = 1; i <= pages; i++) {
    44→        await poppler.pdfToCairo(res.data, filename, {
    45→            firstPageToConvert: i,
    46→            lastPageToConvert: i,
    47→            pngFile: true,
    48→        })
    49→
    50→        const path = getPDFPageImagePath(filename, i)
    51→        const imgFile = fs.readFileSync(path)
    52→        const uploadPageFile = await getPresignedUrlForUpload(`${filename}-${i}.png`)
    53→        const instance = axios.create()
    54→        await instance.put(uploadPageFile.url, imgFile)
    55→        images.push(uploadPageFile.filename)
    56→        fs.unlinkSync(path)
    57→    }
    58→
    59→    return images
    60→
    61→}
    62→
                      ```

                                       1→import { getPresignedUrlForGet } from "@/lib/file"
     2→import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma"
     3→import { convertPdfToImages } from "@/lib/pdf"
     4→import { transcribeAudio } from "./transcribe"
     5→import { imageToMarkdown } from "./imageToMarkdown"
     6→import { sendEmail } from "@/lib/email"
     7→import { indexTextToVectorDB } from "./memory"
     8→import { logger } from "@/lib/logger"
     9→
    10→export async function indexSource(sourceId: string) {
    11→
    12→    const source = await prisma.source.findFirstOrThrow({
    13→        where: {
    14→            id: sourceId
    15→        }
    16→    })
    17→
    18→    try {
    19→        const file = await getPresignedUrlForGet(source.fileName)
    20→
    21→        // PDF
    22→        if (source.fileType === "application/pdf") {
    23→
    24→            const images = await convertPdfToImages(file.url, source.fileName)
    25→
    26→            for (const image of images) {
    27→
    28→                const imageFile = await getPresignedUrlForGet(image)
    29→                const referenceText = await imageToMarkdown(imageFile.url)
    30→                const indexId = await indexTextToVectorDB(referenceText, source.organizationId, source.id)
    31→
    32→                await prisma.indexedSource.create({
    33→                    data: {
    34→                        indexId: indexId,
    35→                        referenceImageFileName: imageFile.filename,
    36→                        sourceId: source.id,
    37→                        organizationId: source.organizationId,
    38→                        referenceText: referenceText
    39→                    }
    40→                })
    41→            }
    42→        }
    43→
    44→        // Image
    45→        if (source.fileType === "image/jpeg" || source.fileType === "image/jpg" || source.fileType === "image/png") {
    46→            const referenceText = await imageToMarkdown(file.url)
    47→            const indexId = await indexTextToVectorDB(referenceText, source.organizationId, source.id)
    48→            await prisma.indexedSource.create({
    49→                data: {
    50→                    indexId: indexId,
    51→                    referenceImageFileName: source.fileName,
    52→                    sourceId: source.id,
    53→                    organizationId: source.organizationId,
    54→                    referenceText: referenceText
    55→                }
    56→            })
    57→        }
    58→
    59→        // Audio
    60→        if (source.fileType === "audio/mp3") {
    61→            const transcription = await transcribeAudio(file.url)
    62→
    63→            const indexId = await indexTextToVectorDB(transcription, source.organizationId, source.id)
    64→
    65→            await prisma.indexedSource.create({
    66→                data: {
    67→                    indexId: indexId,
    68→                    referenceText: transcription,
    69→                    sourceId: source.id,
    70→                    organizationId: source.organizationId
    71→                }
    72→            })
    73→
    74→        }
    75→
    76→        // Video
    77→        if (source.fileType === "video/mp4") {
    78→            // TODO: Index Video
    79→        }
    80→
    81→        // Docx
    82→        if (source.fileType === "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document") {
    83→            // TODO: Index Docx
    84→        }
    85→
    86→
    87→        logger.info(`The source ${source.id} has been indexed`)
    88→
    89→        if (source.uploadedById) {
    90→
    91→            const user = await prisma.user.findFirstOrThrow({
    92→                where: {
    93→                    id: source.uploadedById
    94→                }
    95→            })
    96→
    97→            await sendEmail(
    98→                user.email,
    99→                "Source Indexed",
   100→                `The source you uploaded, ${source.nickName} has been indexed.`
   101→            )
   102→
   103→        }
   104→
   105→
   106→        await prisma.source.update({
   107→            where: {
   108→                id: sourceId
   109→            },
   110→            data: {
   111→                isIndexing: false,
   112→                isIndexed: true,
   113→                indexRunCount: {
   114→                    increment: 1
   115→                }
   116→            }
   117→        })
   118→
   119→    } catch (err) {
   120→
   121→        await prisma.source.update({
   122→            where: {
   123→                id: sourceId
   124→            },
   125→            data: {
   126→                isIndexing: false,
   127→                isIndexed: false,
   128→                indexRunCount: {
   129→                    increment: 1
   130→                }
   131→            }
   132→        })
   133→
   134→        logger.error(`Failed to index source of ${sourceId}`)
   135→        logger.error(err)
   136→    }
   137→}
   138→
                                  ```

                                      ```
                                           1→import axios from "axios"
     2→import { OpenAI } from "openai"
     3→
     4→export async function imageToMarkdown(url: string) {
     5→
     6→    const openai = new OpenAI({
     7→        apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
     8→    })
     9→
    10→    const response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
    11→        model: "gpt-4o-mini",
    12→        messages: [
    13→            {
    14→                role: "system",
    15→                content: "You are a helpful assistant that converts images to markdown."
    16→            },
    17→            {
    18→                role: "user",
    19→                content: [
    20→                    {
    21→                        type: "text",
    22→                        text: `
    23→                            Convert the text in the image to markdown. 
    24→                            If any charts are present, convert them to tables. 
    25→                            For images generate description of the image.    
    26→                        `
    27→                    },
    28→                    {
    29→                        type: "image_url",
    30→                        image_url: {
    31→                            "url": url,
    32→                        },
    33→                    },
    34→                ],
    35→            }
    36→        ]
    37→    })
    38→
    39→    return response.choices[0].message.content || ""
    40→}
    41→
                                      ```

                                               1→import { sendEmail } from "@/lib/email"
     2→import { logger } from "@/lib/logger"
     3→import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma"
     4→import { OpenAI } from "openai"
     5→import { zodResponseFormat } from "openai/helpers/zod"
     6→import { z } from "zod"
     7→import { checkCredits, consumeCredits } from "./ee/billing"
     8→
     9→export async function extractTableToSheet(sheetId: string) {
    10→    const sheet = await prisma.sheet.findFirstOrThrow({
    11→        where: {
    12→            id: sheetId,
    13→            extractInProgress: true
    14→        },
    15→        include: {
    16→            sheetSources: true,
    17→            createdBy: true
    18→        }
    19→    })
    20→
    21→    if (!sheet) {
    22→        throw new Error("Sheet not found");
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    if (sheet.sheetSources.length !== 1) {
    26→        throw new Error("Sheet must have exactly one source");
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    try {
    30→
    31→        const columns = await prisma.sheetColumn.findMany({
    32→            where: {
    33→                sheetId: sheetId
    34→            }
    35→        })
    36→
    37→        // Delete all rows in the sheet
    38→        await prisma.extractedSheetRow.deleteMany({
    39→            where: {
    40→                sheetId: sheetId
    41→            }
    42→        })
    43→
    44→        const openai = new OpenAI({
    45→            apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    46→        })
    47→
    48→        const indexedSources = await prisma.indexedSource.findMany({
    49→            where: {
    50→                sourceId: sheet.sheetSources[0].sourceId
    51→            }
    52→        })
    53→
    54→        const rowSchema = z.object(
    55→            Object.fromEntries(
    56→                columns.map((column) => [
    57→                    column.name,
    58→                    z.string().optional().describe(column.instruction)
    59→                ])
    60→            )
    61→        )
    62→
    63→
    64→        if (process.env.EE_ENABLED && process.env.EE_ENABLED === "true") {
    65→            const creditsAvailable = await checkCredits(sheet.organizationId, indexedSources.length * 10)
    66→            if (!creditsAvailable) {
    67→                throw new Error("No credits available")
    68→            }
    69→        }
    70→
    71→        for (let indexedSource of indexedSources) {
    72→            const response = await openai.beta.chat.completions.parse({
    73→                model: "gpt-4o-mini",
    74→                messages: [
    75→                    {
    76→                        role: "system",
    77→                        content: "You need to extract data from a tables or charts into rows of data"
    78→                    },
    79→                    {
    80→                        role: "user",
    81→                        content: indexedSource.referenceText || ""
    82→                    }
    83→                ],
    84→                temperature: 0,
    85→                response_format: zodResponseFormat(
    86→                    z.object({
    87→                        rows: z.array(rowSchema)
    88→                    }),
    89→                    "rows"
    90→                )
    91→            })
    92→
    93→            const output = response.choices[0].message.parsed
    94→
    95→            let rowCount = 1
    96→
    97→            // logger.info(JSON.stringify(output))
    98→
    99→            if (output) {
   100→
   101→                for (let row of output.rows) {
   102→
   103→                    for (let columnName in row) {
   104→                        const foundColumn = columns.find((column) => column.name === columnName)
   105→
   106→                        if (foundColumn) {
   107→
   108→                            await prisma.extractedSheetRow.create({
   109→                                data: {
   110→                                    sheetId: sheetId,
   111→                                    sheetColumnId: foundColumn.id,
   112→                                    rowNumber: rowCount,
   113→                                    organizationId: sheet.organizationId,
   114→                                    value: row[columnName],
   115→                                    indexedSourceId: indexedSource.id
   116→                                }
   117→                            })
   118→                        }
   119→                    }
   120→
   121→                    rowCount += 1
   122→                }
   123→            }
   124→        }
   125→
   126→        if (process.env.EE_ENABLED && process.env.EE_ENABLED === "true") {
   127→            await consumeCredits(sheet.organizationId, indexedSources.length * 10)
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        await prisma.sheet.update({
   131→            where: {
   132→                id: sheetId
   133→            },
   134→            data: {
   135→                extractInProgress: false
   136→            }
   137→        })
   138→
   139→        if (sheet.createdBy) {
   140→
   141→            await sendEmail(
   142→                sheet.createdBy.email,
   143→                "Extraction Complete",
   144→                `The extraction for sheet ${sheet.name} has been completed. You can now view the data in the sheet.`
   145→            )
   146→        }
   147→
   148→    } catch (err) {
   149→        logger.error(err)
   150→
   151→        await prisma.sheet.update({
   152→            where: {
   153→                id: sheetId
   154→            },
   155→            data: {
   156→                extractInProgress: false
   157→            }
   158→        })
   159→
   160→        if (sheet.createdBy) {
   161→            await sendEmail(
   162→                sheet.createdBy.email,
   163→                "Extraction Failed",
   164→                `The extraction for sheet ${sheet.name} has failed. Please try again later.`
   165→            )
   166→        }
   167→    }
   168→
   169→}
   170→
                                          ```

                                                ```
                                                     1→import { getWeaviateClient } from "@/lib/weaviate"
     2→import { OpenAI } from "openai"
     3→import { WeaviateClient } from "weaviate-client"
     4→
     5→
     6→async function createTenantOrCollectionIfNotExists(client: WeaviateClient, collectionName: string, organizationId: string) {
     7→
     8→    const collectionExists = await client.collections.exists(collectionName)
     9→
    10→    if (!collectionExists) {
    11→        await client.collections.create({
    12→            name: collectionName,
    13→            multiTenancy: {
    14→                enabled: true
    15→            },
    16→            properties: [
    17→                {
    18→                    name: "source",
    19→                    dataType: "text",
    20→                    indexFilterable: true,
    21→                    indexSearchable: true,
    22→                    tokenization: "word"
    23→                }
    24→            ]
    25→        })
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    const tenant = await client.collections.get(collectionName).tenants.getByName(organizationId)
    29→
    30→    if (!tenant) {
    31→        await client.collections.get(collectionName).tenants.create({
    32→            name: organizationId,
    33→            activityStatus: "ACTIVE"
    34→        })
    35→    }
    36→}
    37→
    38→
    39→export async function indexTextToVectorDB(text: string, organizationId: string, sourceId: string) {
    40→
    41→    const client = await getWeaviateClient()
    42→
    43→    await createTenantOrCollectionIfNotExists(client, "Documents", organizationId)
    44→
    45→    const collection = client.collections.get("Documents").withTenant(organizationId)
    46→
    47→    const openai = new OpenAI({
    48→        apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    49→    })
    50→
    51→    const embedding = await openai.embeddings.create({
    52→        input: text,
    53→        model: "text-embedding-3-small"
    54→    })
    55→
    56→    const result = await collection.data.insert({
    57→        properties: {
    58→            source: sourceId
    59→        },
    60→        vectors: embedding.data[0].embedding,
    61→    })
    62→
    63→    return result
    64→
    65→}
    66→
    67→export async function queryVectorDB(text: string, organizationId: string, sourceId: string | null = null) {
    68→    const client = await getWeaviateClient()
    69→
    70→    await createTenantOrCollectionIfNotExists(client, "Documents", organizationId)
    71→
    72→    const collection = client.collections.get("Documents").withTenant(organizationId)
    73→
    74→    const openai = new OpenAI({
    75→        apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    76→    })
    77→
    78→    const embedding = await openai.embeddings.create({
    79→        input: text,
    80→        model: "text-embedding-3-small"
    81→    })
    82→
    83→    const result = await collection.query.nearVector(embedding.data[0].embedding, {
    84→        limit: 1,
    85→        filters: sourceId ? {
    86→            target: {
    87→                property: "source"
    88→            },
    89→            operator: "Equal",
    90→            value: sourceId
    91→        } : undefined
    92→    })
    93→
    94→    return result.objects.length > 0 ? result.objects[0].uuid : ""
    95→}
    96→
    97→
    98→export async function deleteVectorIndex(indexId: string, organizationId: string) {
    99→    
   100→    const client = await getWeaviateClient()
   101→
   102→    await createTenantOrCollectionIfNotExists(client, "Documents", organizationId)
   103→
   104→    const collection = client.collections.get("Documents").withTenant(organizationId)
   105→
   106→    await collection.data.deleteById(indexId)
   107→
   108→    return
   109→}
   110→
                                                ```

                                                         1→import axios from "axios"
     2→import { OpenAI } from "openai"

     4→export async function transcribeAudio(url: string) {
     5→
     6→    const openai = new OpenAI({
     7→        apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
     8→    })
     9→
    10→    const res = await axios.get(url, { responseType: 'arraybuffer' })

    17→    return transcription.text
    18→
    19→}

                                                              ## 👤 user (10:28:04)

                                                              ✅ **Result:**
                                                              ```
                                                                   1→import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma"
     2→import { readYamlFile } from "./yaml"
     3→import axios from "axios"
     4→import { mapData } from "./mapper"
     5→import { validate } from "jsonschema"
     6→
     7→async function getAccessToken(toolName: string, organizationId: string) {
     8→    const tool = await prisma.integration.findFirst({
     9→        where: { name: toolName, organizationId: organizationId }
    10→    })
    11→
    12→    if (!tool) {
    13→        throw new Error("Tool not found")
    14→    }
    15→
    16→    const toolsFile = readYamlFile("tools.yml")
    17→    const toolData = toolsFile.tools.find((tool: any) => tool.name === toolName)
    18→
    19→
    20→    if (tool.refreshToken && tool.accessTokenExpiresAt && tool.accessTokenExpiresAt < new Date()) {
    21→
    22→        const data = new URLSearchParams({
    23→            grant_type: "refresh_token",
    24→            refresh_token: tool.refreshToken,
    25→            client_id: eval(toolData.auth_info.client_id),
    26→            client_secret: eval(toolData.auth_info.client_secret)
    27→        })
    28→
    29→        const response = await axios({
    30→            method: "POST",
    31→            url: eval(toolData.auth_info.token_url),
    32→            data: data.toString(),
    33→            headers: {
    34→                "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
    35→            }
    36→        })
    37→
    38→        tool.accessToken = response.data.access_token
    39→        tool.accessTokenExpiresAt = new Date(new Date().getTime() + response.data.expires_in * 1000)
    40→
    41→        await prisma.integration.update({
    42→            where: {
    43→                name_organizationId: {
    44→                    name: toolName,
    45→                    organizationId: organizationId
    46→                }
    47→            },
    48→            data: tool
    49→        })
    50→    }
    51→
    52→    return tool.accessToken
    53→
    54→}
    55→
    56→
    57→export async function getAvailableTools(organizationId: string) {
    58→    const toolsFile = readYamlFile("tools.yml")
    59→    const toolsList = toolsFile.tools.map((tool: any) => ({ name: tool.name, auth: tool.auth, configured: false }))
    60→
    61→    // For each tool, check if the ownerId has the tool from prisma
    62→    for (const index in toolsList) {
    63→        const existingTool = await prisma.integration.findFirst({
    64→            where: {
    65→                name: toolsList[index].name,
    66→                organizationId: organizationId
    67→            }
    68→        })
    69→
    70→        if (existingTool) {
    71→            toolsList[index].configured = true
    72→        }
    73→    }
    74→
    75→    return toolsList
    76→}
    77→
                                                              ```

                                                                  ## 👤 user (10:28:05)

                                                                  ✅ **Result:**
                                                                  ```
                                                                       1→import { OpenAI } from "openai"
     2→import { ChatCompletionMessageParam } from "openai/resources/index.mjs"
     3→
     4→export async function generateAnswer(
     5→    query: string,
     6→    imageUrls: Array<string> = [],
     7→    history: Array<{ role: "user" | "assistant", content: string }> = []
     8→) {
     9→    const openai = new OpenAI({
    10→        apiKey: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
    11→    })
    12→
    13→    let messages: Array<ChatCompletionMessageParam> = []
    14→
    15→    messages.push({
    16→        role: "system",
    17→        content: `Answer the question based on the data${imageUrls.length > 0 ? " and the image" : ""}`
    18→    })
    19→
    20→    if(history.length > 0) {
    21→        history.forEach(message => {
    22→            messages.push(message)
    23→        })
    24→    }
    25→
    26→    if (imageUrls.length > 0) {
    27→        imageUrls.forEach(image => {
    28→            messages.push({
    29→                role: "user",
    30→                content: [
    31→                    {
    32→                        type: "image_url",
    33→                        image_url: {
    34→                            url: image
    35→                        }
    36→                    }
    37→                ]
    38→            })
    39→        })
    40→    }
    41→
    42→    messages.push({
    43→        role: "user",
    44→        content: query
    45→    })
    46→
    47→    const response = await openai.chat.completions.create({
    48→        model: "gpt-4o-mini",
    49→        messages: messages
    50→    })
    51→
    52→    return response.choices[0].message.content || ""
    53→}
    54→
                                                                  ```

                                                                           1→import fs from "fs"
     2→import yaml from "js-yaml"
     3→
     4→// Helper to read YAML files
     5→export const readYamlFile = (filename: string) => {
     6→    const fileContents = fs.readFileSync(filename, "utf8")
     7→    return yaml.load(fileContents) as any
     8→}

                                                                                 1→<div align="center">
     2→  <img src="public/logo-full.svg" alt="Rowfill Logo" width="200">
     3→  <p>Open-source agentic tables platform for deep research and document analysis</p>
     4→</div>
     5→
     6→## 👋 Welcome to Rowfill
     7→
     8→Rowfill is an open-source platform that transforms how you work with data through agentic tables. It combines powerful document extraction, deep research capabilities, and intelligent data processing to help you uncover insights from any data source.
     9→
    10→## ✨ Features
    11→
    12→### 🤖 Agentic Tables
    13→- **AI-Powered Analysis**: Tables that understand context and can reason about your data
    14→- **Autonomous Research**: Automatically extract, analyze, and synthesize information from multiple sources
    15→- **Dynamic Insights**: Real-time data enrichment and intelligent suggestions as you work
    16→
    17→### 📚 Deep Research & Extraction
    18→- **Multi-Source Processing**: Extract data from documents, PDFs, images, web content, and databases
    19→- **Semantic Understanding**: Advanced AI models that comprehend document context and relationships
    20→- **Structured Output**: Automatically organize extracted information into meaningful table structures
    21→- **Cross-Reference Analysis**: Connect and verify information across multiple sources
    22→
    23→### 🔄 Data Processing
    24→- **Intelligent Schema Detection**: Automatically adapt to and understand different data structures
    25→- **Custom Workflows**: Create sophisticated data processing pipelines with AI assistance
    26→- **Version Control**: Track changes and maintain data lineage
    27→- **Export & Integration**: Seamlessly export to various formats and integrate with your tools
    28→
    29→### 🔒 Privacy & Security
    30→- **Local LLM Support**: Run with Local LLMs like Llama and Mistral, or use OpenAI vision models
    31→- **Data Sovereignty**: Keep sensitive data within your infrastructure
    32→- **Open Source**: AGPLv3 licensed for transparency and customization
    33→
    34→## 🚀 Getting Started
    35→
    36→1. Clone the repository
    37→2. Run the docker compose file
    38→3. Configure environment variables (see mockenv file)
    39→4. Start exploring your data with agentic tables!
    40→
    41→## 📚 Documentation
    42→
    43→Visit our [documentation](https://docs.rowfill.com) for:
    44→- Comprehensive guides and tutorials
    45→- API reference
    46→- Advanced features documentation
    47→- Integration examples
    48→- Best practices for data analysis
    49→
    50→## 🤝 Contributing
    51→
    52→We welcome contributions! Here's how you can help:
    53→
    54→1. Fork the repository
    55→2. Create your feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`)
    56→3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m 'Add some AmazingFeature'`)
    57→4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/AmazingFeature`)
    58→5. Open a Pull Request
    59→
    60→## 📝 License
    61→
    62→This project is licensed under the AGPLV3 License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.
    63→
    64→## 💬 Community & Support
    65→
    66→Join our growing community:
    67→- [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/y65HPrCe)
    68→- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/harishdeivanayagam/rowfill/issues)
    69→- [Email Support](mailto:hello@rowfill.com)
    70→
    71→## 🖥 Cloud Version
    72→
    73→Try Rowfill Cloud (Alpha) at [Rowfill Cloud](https://cloud.rowfill.com)
    74→
    75→For early access, [contact us](mailto:support@rowfill.com)
    76→
    77→---
    78→**Note**: This project is under active development. While core features are stable, we're continuously adding new capabilities and improvements.
    79→
    80→---
    81→<div align="center">

    83→</div>

                                                                                    🔧 **Tool:** Read

  "file_path": "/home/julien/Documents/IACA/rowfill/prisma/schema.prisma"

                                                                                      ## 👤 user (10:28:11)

                                                                                      ✅ **Result:**
                                                                                      ```
                                                                                           1→generator client {
     2→  provider = "prisma-client-js"
     3→}
     4→
     5→datasource db {
     6→  provider = "postgresql"
     7→  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
     8→}
     9→
    10→enum Role {
    11→  ADMIN
    12→  MEMBER
    13→}
    14→
    15→// ONLY for EE Usage
    16→enum Plan {
    17→  FREE
    18→  PRO
    19→  ENTERPRISE
    20→}
    21→
    22→enum AuthType {
    23→  OAUTH2
    24→  APIKEY
    25→}
    26→
    27→model User {
    28→  id            String    @id @default(cuid())
    29→  name          String?
    30→  email         String    @unique
    31→  emailVerified DateTime?
    32→  otp           String?
    33→  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())
    34→  updatedAt     DateTime  @updatedAt
    35→  members       Member[]
    36→  sheets        Sheet[]
    37→}
    38→
    39→model Organization {
    40→  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
    41→  name      String
    42→  apiKey    String   @unique @default(uuid())
    43→  mcpTools  Json     @default("[]")
    44→  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
    45→  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
    46→  members   Member[]
    47→  sheets    Sheet[]
    48→  billing   Billing?
    49→  columns   Column[]
    50→}
    51→
    52→model Member {
    53→  organizationId String
    54→  organization   Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id])
    55→  userId         String
    56→  user           User         @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
    57→  role           Role
    58→
    59→  @@id([userId, organizationId])
    60→}
    61→
    62→// ONLY for EE Usage
    63→model Billing {
    64→  organizationId String       @id @unique
    65→  organization   Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id])
    66→  credits        Int          @default(1000) // 1 credit = 1 Column Task or 10 Credits for 1 row extraction
    67→  thirdPartyId   String?
    68→  subscriptionId String?
    69→  plan           Plan         @default(FREE)
    70→  expiresAt      DateTime?
    71→  createdAt      DateTime     @default(now())
    72→  updatedAt      DateTime     @updatedAt
    73→}
    74→
    75→model Sheet {
    76→  id                String       @id @default(uuid())
    77→  name              String
    78→  createdById       String?
    79→  createdBy         User?        @relation(fields: [createdById], references: [id])
    80→  organizationId    String
    81→  organization      Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id])
    82→  singleSource      Boolean      @default(false)
    83→  extractInProgress Boolean      @default(false)
    84→  createdAt         DateTime     @default(now())
    85→  updatedAt         DateTime     @updatedAt
    86→  columns           Column[]
    87→  cells             Json         @default("{}")  // row_column: value
    88→}
    89→
    90→model Column {
    91→  id             String       @id @default(uuid())
    92→  sheetId        String
    93→  sheet          Sheet        @relation(fields: [sheetId], references: [id])
    94→  name           String
    95→  organizationId String
    96→  organization   Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id])
    97→  position       Int
    98→  createdAt      DateTime     @default(now())
    99→  updatedAt      DateTime     @updatedAt
   100→}
   101→
                                                                                      ```

     6→    private _queue: Queue;

    23→        if (!TaskQueue._instance) {

    25→        }
